This module implements:\n4\t//! - Write deduplication via idempotency keys\n5\t//! - Read query coalescing for identical concurrent searches\n6\t\n7\tuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\n8\tuse RwLock};\n13\t\n14\t/// Idempotency cache entry.\n15\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n16\tpub struct IdempotencyEntry {\n17\t    /// SHA256 hash of the request body.\n18\t    pub body_hash: String,\n19\t    /// Miroir task ID returned to the client.\n20\t    pub mtask_id: String,\n21\t    /// Expiration timestamp.\n22\t    pub expires_at: u64,\n23\t}\n24\t\n25\t/// Idempotency cache for write deduplication.\n26\tpub struct IdempotencyCache {\n27\t    /// key -> (body_hash, mtask_id, expires_at)\n28\t    cache: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, IdempotencyEntry>>>,\n29\t    /// Maximum entries (LRU cap).\n30\t    max_entries: usize,\n31\t    /// TTL in seconds.\n32\t    ttl_secs: u64,\n33\t}\n34\t\n35\timpl IdempotencyCache {\n36\t    /// Create a new idempotency cache.\n37\t    pub fn new(max_entries: usize, ttl_secs: u64) -> Self {\n38\t        Self {\n39\t            cache: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n40\t            max_entries,\n41\t            ttl_secs,\n42\t        }\n43\t    }\n44\t\n45\t    /// Check for a duplicate write.\n46\t    ///\n47\t    /// Returns `Some(mtask_id)` if the key exists and body hash matches.\n48\t    /// Returns `Err` if the key exists but body hash differs.\n49\t    /// Returns `None` if the key is not found (caller should process).\n50\t    pub async fn check(&self, key: &str, body_hash: &str) -> Result<Option<String>> {\n51\t        let mut cache = self.cache.write().await;\n52\t        self.prune_expired_locked(&mut cache);\n53\t\n54\t        if let Some(entry) = cache.get(key) {\n55\t            if entry.body_hash == body_hash {\n56\t                return Ok(Some(entry.mtask_id.clone()));\n57\t            } else {\n58\t                return Err(MiroirError::IdempotencyKeyReused);\n59\t            }\n60\t        }\n61\t        Ok(None)\n62\t    }\n63\t\n64\t    /// Insert a new idempotency entry.\n65\t    pub async fn insert(&self, key: String, body_hash: String, mtask_id: String) {\n66\t        let mut cache = self.cache.write().await;\n67\t        self.prune_expired_locked(&mut cache);\n68\t\n69\t        // Enforce max entries (simple FIFO for now)\n70\t        if cache.len() >= self.max_entries {\n71\t            // Remove oldest entry (first key)\n72\t            if let Some(k) = cache.keys().next().cloned() {\n73\t                cache.remove(&k);\n74\t            }\n75\t        }\n76\t\n77\t        let now = SystemTime::now()\n78\t            .duration_since(SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH)\n79\t            .unwrap_or_default()\n80\t            .as_secs();\n81\t        let expires_at = now + self.ttl_secs;\n82\t\n83\t        cache.insert(key, IdempotencyEntry {\n84\t            body_hash,\n85\t            mtask_id,\n86\t            expires_at,\n87\t        });\n88\t    }\n89\t\n90\t    /// Prune expired entries (must hold write lock).\n91\t    fn prune_expired_locked(&self, cache: &mut HashMap<String, IdempotencyEntry>) {\n92\t        let now = SystemTime::now()\n93\t            .duration_since(SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH)\n94\t            .unwrap_or_default()\n95\t            .as_secs();\n96\t        cache.retain(|_, v| v.expires_at > now);\n97\t    }\n98\t\n99\t    /// Get current cache size.\n100\t    pub async fn size(&self) -> usize {\n101\t        self.cache.read().await.len()\n102\t    }\n103\t}\n104\t\n105\t/// Fingerprint of a query for coalescing.\n106\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\n107\tpub struct QueryFingerprint {\n108\t    /// Index UID.\n109\t    pub index: String,\n110\t    /// Canonical JSON of search query.\n111\t    pub query_json: String,\n112\t    /// Settings version (include to avoid coalescing across settings changes).\n113\t    pub settings_version: u64,\n114\t}\n115\t\n116\timpl QueryFingerprint {\n117\t    /// Create a new query fingerprint from index, query body, and settings version.\n118\t    ///\n119\t    /// Canonicalizes the JSON to ensure different key orders produce the same fingerprint.\n120\t    pub fn new(index: String, query_body: &serde_json::Value, settings_version: u64) -> Self {\n121\t        // Canonicalize JSON: sort keys recursively to ensure consistent fingerprint\n122\t        let query_json = canonical_json(query_body);\n123\t\n124\t        Self {\n125\t            index,\n126\t            query_json,\n127\t            settings_version,\n128\t        }\n129\t    }\n130\t}\n131\t\n132\t/// Canonicalize a JSON value by sorting all object keys recursively.\n133\tfn canonical_json(value: &serde_json::Value) -> String {\n134\t    match value {\n135\t        serde_json::Value::Object(map) => {\n136\t            let mut sorted_map = serde_json::Map::new();\n137\t            let mut sorted_keys: Vec<&String> = map.keys().collect();\n138\t            sorted_keys.sort();\n139\t\n140\t            for key in sorted_keys {\n141\t                let canonical_value = canonical_json(&map[key]);\n142\t                sorted_map.insert(key.clone(), serde_json::from_str(&canonical_value).unwrap());\n143\t            }\n144\t\n145\t            serde_json::to_string(&sorted_map).unwrap_or_else(|_| value.to_string())\n146\t        }\n147\t        serde_json::Value::Array(arr) => {\n148\t            let canonical_arr: Vec<String> = arr.iter().map(canonical_json).collect();\n149\t            serde_json::to_string(&canonical_arr).unwrap_or_else(|_| value.to_string())\n150\t        }\n151\t        _ => value.to_string(),\n152\t    }\n153\t}\n154\t\n155\t/// Pending query state for coalescing.\n156\tpub struct PendingQuery {\n157\t    /// Response broadcast channel.\n158\t    tx: broadcast::Sender<Vec<u8>>,\n159\t    /// When this query was initiated.\n160\t    started_at: Instant,\n161\t}\n162\t\n163\t/// Query coalescing cache.\n164\tpub struct QueryCoalescer {\n165\t    /// Fingerprint -> pending query state.\n166\t    pending: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<QueryFingerprint, max_subscribers: usize) -> Self {\n178\t        Self {\n179\t            pending: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n180\t            window_ms,\n181\t            max_pending,\n182\t            max_subscribers,\n183\t        }\n184\t    }\n185\t\n186\t    /// Check if a query can be coalesced.\n187\t    ///\n188\t    /// Returns `Some(receiver)` if a matching query is in flight.\n189\t    /// Returns `None` if no matching query (caller should dispatch).\n190\t    pub async fn try_coalesce(\n191\t        &self,\n192\t        fingerprint: QueryFingerprint,\n193\t    ) -> Option<broadcast::Receiver<Vec<u8>>> {\n194\t        let mut pending = self.pending.write().await;\n195\t        self.prune_stale_locked(&mut pending);\n196\t\n197\t        if let Some(pq) = pending.get(&fingerprint) {\n198\t            if pq.tx.receiver_count() < self.max_subscribers {\n199\t                return Some(pq.tx.subscribe());\n200\t            }\n201\t        }\n202\t        None\n203\t    }\n204\t\n205\t    /// Register a new pending query.\n206\t    ///\n207\t    /// Returns a broadcast sender that the caller should notify on completion.\n208\t    pub async fn register(\n209\t        &self,\n210\t        fingerprint: QueryFingerprint,\n211\t    ) -> Result<broadcast::Sender<Vec<u8>>> {\n212\t        let mut pending = self.pending.write().await;\n213\t        self.prune_stale_locked(&mut pending);\n214\t\n215\t        if pending.len() >= self.max_pending {\n216\t            return Err(MiroirError::TooManyPendingQueries);\n217\t        }\n218\t\n219\t        let (tx, _) = broadcast::channel(self.max_subscribers);\n220\t        pending.insert(fingerprint, PendingQuery {\n221\t            tx: tx.clone(),\n222\t            started_at: Instant::now(),\n223\t        });\n224\t        Ok(tx)\n225\t    }\n226\t\n227\t    /// Unregister a pending query after completion.\n228\t    pub async fn unregister(&self, fingerprint: &QueryFingerprint) {\n229\t        let mut pending = self.pending.write().await;\n230\t        pending.remove(fingerprint);\n231\t    }\n232\t\n233\t    /// Prune stale entries older than the window (must hold write lock).\n234\t    fn prune_stale_locked(&self, pending: &mut HashMap<QueryFingerprint, PendingQuery>) {\n235\t        let window = Duration::from_millis(self.window_ms);\n236\t        let now = Instant::now();\n237\t        pending.retain(|_, pq| now.duration_since(pq.started_at) < window);\n238\t    }\n239\t\n240\t    /// Get current pending count.\n241\t    pub async fn pending_count(&self) -> usize {\n242\t        self.pending.read().await.len()\n243\t    }\n244\t}\n245\t\n246\t#[cfg(test)]\n247\tmod tests {\n248\t    use super::*;\n249\t\n250\t    #[tokio::test]\n251\t    async fn test_idempotency_miss_then_hit() {\n252\t        let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(100, 3600);\n253\t\n254\t        // First check should miss.\n255\t        let result = cache.check(\"key1\", \"hash1\").await.unwrap();\n256\t        assert!(result.is_none());\n257\t\n258\t        // Insert.\n259\t        cache.insert(\"key1\".into(), \"hash1\".into(), \"mtask-1\".into()).await;\n260\t\n261\t        // Second check should hit.\n262\t        let result = cache.check(\"key1\", \"hash1\").await.unwrap();\n263\t        assert_eq!(result, Some(\"mtask-1\".into()));\n264\t    }\n265\t\n266\t    #[tokio::test]\n267\t    async fn test_idempotency_conflict() {\n268\t        let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(100, 3600);\n269\t\n270\t        cache.insert(\"key1\".into(), \"hash1\".into(), \"mtask-1\".into()).await;\n271\t\n272\t        // Different body hash should error.\n273\t        let result = cache.check(\"key1\", \"hash2\").await;\n274\t        assert!(matches!(result, Err(MiroirError::IdempotencyKeyReused)));\n275\t    }\n276\t\n277\t    #[tokio::test]\n278\t    async fn test_idempotency_max_entries() {\n279\t        let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(3, 3600);\n280\t\n281\t        cache.insert(\"key1\".into(), \"hash1\".into(), \"mtask-1\".into()).await;\n282\t        cache.insert(\"key2\".into(), \"hash2\".into(), \"mtask-2\".into()).await;\n283\t        cache.insert(\"key3\".into(), \"hash3\".into(), \"mtask-3\".into()).await;\n284\t\n285\t        // At max.\n286\t        assert_eq!(cache.size().await, 3);\n287\t\n288\t        // Adding a 4th should evict some entry to maintain max size.\n289\t        cache.insert(\"key4\".into(), \"hash4\".into(), \"mtask-4\".into()).await;\n290\t        assert_eq!(cache.size().await, 3);\n291\t        // At least one of the original keys should be evicted.\n292\t        let remaining = [\n293\t            cache.check(\"key1\", \"hash1\").await.unwrap().is_some(),\n294\t            cache.check(\"key2\", \"hash2\").await.unwrap().is_some(),\n295\t            cache.check(\"key3\", \"hash3\").await.unwrap().is_some(),\n296\t        ];\n297\t        assert!(remaining.iter().filter(|&&x| x).count() < 3, \"expected at least one eviction\");\n298\t    }\n299\t\n300\t    #[test]\n301\t    fn test_query_fingerprint_eq() {\n302\t        let fp1 = QueryFingerprint {\n303\t            index: \"products\".into(),\n304\t            query_json: Idempotency keys and query coalescing (plan §13.10).\n//!\n//! This module implements:\n//! - Write deduplication via idempotency keys\n//! - Read query coalescing for identical concurrent searches\n\nuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::{Duration, Instant, SystemTime};\nuse tokio::sync::{broadcast, RwLock};\n\n/// Idempotency cache entry.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct IdempotencyEntry {\n    /// SHA256 hash of the request body.\n    pub body_hash: String,\n    /// Miroir task ID returned to the client.\n    pub mtask_id: String,\n    /// Expiration timestamp.\n    pub expires_at: u64,\n}\n\n/// Idempotency cache for write deduplication.\npub struct IdempotencyCache {\n    /// key -> (body_hash, mtask_id, expires_at)\n    cache: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, IdempotencyEntry>>>,\n    /// Maximum entries (LRU cap).\n    max_entries: usize,\n    /// TTL in seconds.\n    ttl_secs: u64,\n}\n\nimpl IdempotencyCache {\n    /// Create a new idempotency cache.\n    pub fn new(max_entries: usize, ttl_secs: u64) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            cache: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n            max_entries,\n            ttl_secs,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check for a duplicate write.\n    ///\n    /// Returns `Some(mtask_id)` if the key exists and body hash matches.\n    /// Returns `Err` if the key exists but body hash differs.\n    /// Returns `None` if the key is not found (caller should process).\n    pub async fn check(&self, key: &str, body_hash: &str) -> Result<Option<String>> {\n        let mut cache = self.cache.write().await;\n        self.prune_expired_locked(&mut cache);\n\n        if let Some(entry) = cache.get(key) {\n            if entry.body_hash == body_hash {\n                return Ok(Some(entry.mtask_id.clone()));\n            } else {\n                return Err(MiroirError::IdempotencyKeyReused);\n            }\n        }\n        Ok(None)\n    }\n\n    /// Insert a new idempotency entry.\n    pub async fn insert(&self, key: String, body_hash: String, mtask_id: String) {\n        let mut cache = self.cache.write().await;\n        self.prune_expired_locked(&mut cache);\n\n        // Enforce max entries (simple FIFO for now)\n        if cache.len() >= self.max_entries {\n            // Remove oldest entry (first key)\n            if let Some(k) = cache.keys().next().cloned() {\n                cache.remove(&k);\n            }\n        }\n\n        let now = SystemTime::now()\n            .duration_since(SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH)\n            .unwrap_or_default()\n            .as_secs();\n        let expires_at = now + self.ttl_secs;\n\n        cache.insert(key, IdempotencyEntry {\n            body_hash,\n            mtask_id,\n            expires_at,\n        });\n    }\n\n    /// Prune expired entries (must hold write lock).\n    fn prune_expired_locked(&self, cache: &mut HashMap<String, IdempotencyEntry>) {\n        let now = SystemTime::now()\n            .duration_since(SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH)\n            .unwrap_or_default()\n            .as_secs();\n        cache.retain(|_, v| v.expires_at > now);\n    }\n\n    /// Get current cache size.\n    pub async fn size(&self) -> usize {\n        self.cache.read().await.len()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Fingerprint of a query for coalescing.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\npub struct QueryFingerprint {\n    /// Index UID.\n    pub index: String,\n    /// Canonical JSON of search query.\n    pub query_json: String,\n    /// Settings version (include to avoid coalescing across settings changes).\n    pub settings_version: u64,\n}\n\nimpl QueryFingerprint {\n    /// Create a new query fingerprint from index, query body, and settings version.\n    ///\n    /// Canonicalizes the JSON to ensure different key orders produce the same fingerprint.\n    pub fn new(index: String, query_body: &serde_json::Value, settings_version: u64) -> Self {\n        // Canonicalize JSON: sort keys recursively to ensure consistent fingerprint\n        let query_json = canonical_json(query_body);\n\n        Self {\n            index,\n            query_json,\n            settings_version,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Canonicalize a JSON value by sorting all object keys recursively.\nfn canonical_json(value: &serde_json::Value) -> String {\n    match value {\n        serde_json::Value::Object(map) => {\n            let mut sorted_map = serde_json::Map::new();\n            let mut sorted_keys: Vec<&String> = map.keys().collect();\n            sorted_keys.sort();\n\n            for key in sorted_keys {\n                let canonical_value = canonical_json(&map[key]);\n                sorted_map.insert(key.clone(), serde_json::from_str(&canonical_value).unwrap());\n            }\n\n            serde_json::to_string(&sorted_map).unwrap_or_else(|_| value.to_string())\n        }\n        serde_json::Value::Array(arr) => {\n            let canonical_arr: Vec<String> = arr.iter().map(canonical_json).collect();\n            serde_json::to_string(&canonical_arr).unwrap_or_else(|_| value.to_string())\n        }\n        _ => value.to_string(),\n    }\n}\n\n/// Pending query state for coalescing.\npub struct PendingQuery {\n    /// Response broadcast channel.\n    tx: broadcast::Sender<Vec<u8>>,\n    /// When this query was initiated.\n    started_at: Instant,\n}\n\n/// Query coalescing cache.\npub struct QueryCoalescer {\n    /// Fingerprint -> pending query state.\n    pending: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<QueryFingerprint, PendingQuery>>>,\n    /// Coalescing window in milliseconds.\n    window_ms: u64,\n    /// Max concurrent pending queries.\n    max_pending: usize,\n    /// Max subscribers per query.\n    max_subscribers: usize,\n}\n\nimpl QueryCoalescer {\n    /// Create a new query coalescer.\n    pub fn new(window_ms: u64, max_pending: usize, max_subscribers: usize) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            pending: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n            window_ms,\n            max_pending,\n            max_subscribers,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if a query can be coalesced.\n    ///\n    /// Returns `Some(receiver)` if a matching query is in flight.\n    /// Returns `None` if no matching query (caller should dispatch).\n    pub async fn try_coalesce(\n        &self,\n        fingerprint: QueryFingerprint,\n    ) -> Option<broadcast::Receiver<Vec<u8>>> {\n        let mut pending = self.pending.write().await;\n        self.prune_stale_locked(&mut pending);\n\n        if let Some(pq) = pending.get(&fingerprint) {\n            if pq.tx.receiver_count() < self.max_subscribers {\n                return Some(pq.tx.subscribe());\n            }\n        }\n        None\n    }\n\n    /// Register a new pending query.\n    ///\n    /// Returns a broadcast sender that the caller should notify on completion.\n    pub async fn register(\n        &self,\n        fingerprint: QueryFingerprint,\n    ) -> Result<broadcast::Sender<Vec<u8>>> {\n        let mut pending = self.pending.write().await;\n        self.prune_stale_locked(&mut pending);\n\n        if pending.len() >= self.max_pending {\n            return Err(MiroirError::TooManyPendingQueries);\n        }\n\n        let (tx, _) = broadcast::channel(self.max_subscribers);\n        pending.insert(fingerprint, PendingQuery {\n            tx: tx.clone(),\n            started_at: Instant::now(),\n        });\n        Ok(tx)\n    }\n\n    /// Unregister a pending query after completion.\n    pub async fn unregister(&self, fingerprint: &QueryFingerprint) {\n        let mut pending = self.pending.write().await;\n        pending.remove(fingerprint);\n    }\n\n    /// Prune stale entries older than the window (must hold write lock).\n    fn prune_stale_locked(&self, pending: &mut HashMap<QueryFingerprint, PendingQuery>) {\n        let window = Duration::from_millis(self.window_ms);\n        let now = Instant::now();\n        pending.retain(|_, pq| now.duration_since(pq.started_at) < window);\n    }\n\n    /// Get current pending count.\n    pub async fn pending_count(&self) -> usize {\n        self.pending.read().await.len()\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_idempotency_miss_then_hit() {\n        let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(100, 3600);\n\n        // First check should miss.\n        let result = cache.check(\"key1\", \"hash1\").await.unwrap();\n        assert!(result.is_none());\n\n        // Insert.\n        cache.insert(\"key1\".into(), \"hash1\".into(), \"mtask-1\".into()).await;\n\n        // Second check should hit.\n        let result = cache.check(\"key1\", \"hash1\").await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result, Some(\"mtask-1\".into()));\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_idempotency_conflict() {\n        let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(100, 3600);\n\n        cache.insert(\"key1\".into(), \"hash1\".into(), \"mtask-1\".into()).await;\n\n        // Different body hash should error.\n        let result = cache.check(\"key1\", \"hash2\").await;\n        assert!(matches!(result, Err(MiroirError::IdempotencyKeyReused)));\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_idempotency_max_entries() {\n        let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(3, 3600);\n\n        cache.insert(\"key1\".into(), \"hash1\".into(), \"mtask-1\".into()).await;\n        cache.insert(\"key2\".into(), \"hash2\".into(), \"mtask-2\".into()).await;\n        cache.insert(\"key3\".into(), \"hash3\".into(), \"mtask-3\".into()).await;\n\n        // At max.\n        assert_eq!(cache.size().await, 3);\n\n        // Adding a 4th should evict some entry to maintain max size.\n        cache.insert(\"key4\".into(), \"hash4\".into(), \"mtask-4\".into()).await;\n        assert_eq!(cache.size().await, 3);\n        // At least one of the original keys should be evicted.\n        let remaining = [\n            cache.check(\"key1\", \"hash1\").await.unwrap().is_some(),\n            cache.check(\"key2\", \"hash2\").await.unwrap().is_some(),\n            cache.check(\"key3\", \"hash3\").await.unwrap().is_some(),\n        ];\n        assert!(remaining.iter().filter(|&&x| x).count() < 3, \"expected at least one eviction\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_query_fingerprint_eq() {\n        let fp1 = QueryFingerprint {\n            index: \"products\".into(),\n            query_json: r#\"{\"q\":\"laptop\"}\"#.into(),\n            settings_version: 42,\n        };\n        let fp2 = QueryFingerprint {\n            index: \"products\".into(),\n            query_json: r#\"{\"q\":\"laptop\"}\"#.into(),\n            settings_version: 42,\n        };\n        let fp3 = QueryFingerprint {\n            index: \"products\".into(),\n            query_json: r#\"{\"q\":\"phone\"}\"#.into(),\n            settings_version: 42,\n        };\n        assert_eq!(fp1, fp2);\n        assert_ne!(fp1, fp3);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_coalescer_miss_then_register() {\n        let coalescer = QueryCoalescer::new(50, 100, 10);\n        let fp = QueryFingerprint {\n            index: \"products\".into(),\n            query_json: r#\"{\"q\":\"test\"}\"#.into(),\n            settings_version: 1,\n        };\n\n        // Should not find a pending query.\n        assert!(coalescer.try_coalesce(fp.clone()).await.is_none());\n\n        // Register and get sender.\n        let _tx = coalescer.register(fp.clone()).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(coalescer.pending_count().await, 1);\n\n        // Clean up.\n coalescer.try_coalesce(fp).await;\n        assert!(rx.is_some());\n\n        // Broadcast response.\n        let response = b\"test response\".to_vec();\n        tx.send(response.clone()).ok();\n\n {\n82\t    /// Per-group ACK counts: group_id -> successful_ack_count\n83\t    group_acks: HashMap<u32, usize>,\n84\t    /// Per-group total node counts: group_id -> total_nodes_attempted\n85\t *self.group_totals.entry(group_id).or_insert(0) += 1;\n96\t    }\n97\t\n98\t    /// Record a successful write ACK from a node.\n99\t    fn record_success(&mut self, group_id: u32, _node_id: &NodeId) {\n100\t        *self.group_acks.entry(group_id).or_insert(0) += 1;\n101\t    }\n102\t\n103\t    /// Record a failed write attempt from a node.\n104\t    fn record_failure(&mut self, _group_id: u32) {\n105\t        // Track that this group had a failure\n106\t        // Degraded is determined after checking quorum\n107\t    }\n108\t\n109\t    /// Check if a group has met quorum: floor(RF/2) + 1 ACKs required.\n110\t    fn check_group_quorum(&mut self, group_id: u32, rf: usize) -> bool {\n111\t        let acks = *self.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n112\t        let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n113\t        let met = acks >= quorum;\n114\t        *self.groups_met_quorum.entry(group_id).or_insert(false) = met;\n115\t        met\n116\t    }\n117\t\n118\t    /// Count how many groups met quorum.\n119\t    fn count_quorum_groups(&self) -> usize {\n120\t        self.groups_met_quorum.values().filter(|&&v| v).count()\n121\t    }\n122\t\n123\t    /// Count degraded groups (groups that exist but didn't meet quorum).\n124\t    fn count_degraded_groups(&mut self, replica_group_count: u32, rf: usize) -> u32 {\n125\t        let mut degraded = 0u32;\n126\t        for group_id in 0..replica_group_count {\n127\t            if !self.check_group_quorum(group_id, rf) {\n128\t                // Only count as degraded if we attempted to write to this group\n129\t                if self.group_totals.contains_key(&group_id) {\n130\t                    degraded += 1;\n131\t                }\n132\t            }\n133\t        }\n134\t        degraded\n135\t    }\n136\t}\n137\t\n138\t/// Build router for document endpoints.\n139\tpub fn router<S>() -> Router<S>\n140\twhere\n141\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n142\t{\n143\t    Router::new()\n144\t write_documents_impl(index, params.primaryKey, documents, &state, sid, idempotency_key).await\n197\t}\n198\t\n199\t/// DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents - Delete by IDs or filter.\n200\tasync fn delete_documents(\n201\t    Path(index): Path<String>,\n202\t    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n203\t    session_id: Option<Extension<crate::middleware::SessionId>>,\n204\t    headers: axum::http::HeaderMap,\n205\t    Json(body): Json<Value>,\n206\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n207\t    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n208\t        let s = ext.0;\n209\t        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n210\t    });\n211\t\n212\t    // Extract idempotency key (plan §13.10)\n213\t    let idempotency_key = headers\n214\t        .get(\"Idempotency-Key\")\n215\t        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n216\t        .map(|s| s.to_string());\n217\t\n218\t    // Try to parse as delete by filter first\n219\t    if let Some(filter) = body.get(\"filter\") {\n220\t        let req = DeleteByFilterRequest {\n221\t            index_uid: index.clone(),\n222\t            filter: filter.clone(),\n223\t            origin: None, // Client write\n224\t        };\n225\t        return delete_by_filter_impl(index, req, &state, sid, idempotency_key).await;\n226\t    }\n227\t\n228\t    // Try to parse as delete by IDs\n229\t    if let Some(ids) = body.get(\"ids\").and_then(|v| v.as_array()) {\n230\t        let ids: Vec<String> = ids\n231\t            .iter()\n232\t            .filter_map(|v| v.as_str().map(|s| s.to_string()))\n233\t            .collect();\n234\t        if !ids.is_empty() {\n235\t            let req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n236\t                index_uid: index.clone(),\n237\t                ids,\n238\t                origin: None, // Client write\n239\t            };\n240\t            return delete_by_ids_impl(index, req, &state, sid, idempotency_key).await;\n241\t        }\n242\t    }\n243\t\n244\t    // If we get here, the request body is malformed\n245\t    Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n246\t        MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n247\t        \"delete request must include either 'filter' or 'ids' field\",\n248\t    ))\n249\t}\n250\t\n251\t/// DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents/{id} - Delete single document by ID.\n252\tasync fn delete_document_by_id(\n253\t    Path((index, id)): Path<(String, String)>,\n254\t    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n255\t    session_id: Option<Extension<crate::middleware::SessionId>>,\n256\t    headers: axum::http::HeaderMap,\n257\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n258\t    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n259\t        let s = ext.0;\n260\t        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n261\t    });\n262\t\n263\t    // Extract idempotency key (plan §13.10)\n264\t    let idempotency_key = headers\n265\t        .get(\"Idempotency-Key\")\n266\t        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n267\t        .map(|s| s.to_string());\n268\t\n269\t    let req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n270\t        index_uid: index.clone(),\n271\t        ids: vec![id],\n272\t        origin: None, // Client write\n273\t    };\n274\t    delete_by_ids_impl(index, req, &state, sid, idempotency_key).await\n275\t}\n276\t\n277\t/// Implementation for write documents (POST/PUT).\n278\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index, session_id))]\n279\tasync fn write_documents_impl(\n280\t    index: String,\n281\t    primary_key: Option<String>,\n282\t    mut documents: Vec<Value>,\n283\t    state: &AppState,\n284\t    session_id: Option<String>,\n285\t    idempotency_key: Option<String>,\n286\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n287\t    if documents.is_empty() {\n288\t        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n289\t            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n290\t            \"cannot write empty document batch\",\n291\t        ));\n292\t    }\n293\t\n294\t    // 0.5. Check idempotency cache (plan §13.10)\n295\t    if state.config.idempotency.enabled {\n296\t        if let Some(ref key) = idempotency_key {\n297\t            // Compute SHA256 hash of the request body\n298\t            use sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\n299\t            let body_hash = format!(\"{:x}\", Sha256::digest(serde_json::to_string(&documents).unwrap_or_default()));\n300\t\n301\t            // Check cache\n302\t            match state.idempotency_cache.check(key, &body_hash).await {\n303\t                Ok(Some(cached_mtask_id)) => {\n304\t                    // Idempotency hit: return cached mtask ID\n305\t                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"dedup\");\n306\t                    return build_response_with_degraded_header(\n307\t                        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n308\t                            taskUid: Some(cached_mtask_id),\n309\t                            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n310\t                            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n311\t                            error: None,\n312\t                            error_type: None,\n313\t                            code: None,\n314\t                            link: None,\n315\t                        },\n316\t                        0, // No degraded groups for cached response\n317\t                    );\n318\t                }\n319\t                Ok(None) => {\n320\t                    // Cache miss - proceed with processing\n321\t                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"miss\");\n322\t                }\n323\t                Err(miroir_core::error::MiroirError::IdempotencyKeyReused) => {\n324\t                    // Key exists but body hash differs\n325\t                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"conflict\");\n326\t                    return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n327\t                        MiroirCode::IdempotencyKeyReused,\n328\t                        \"idempotency key was already used with a different request body\",\n329\t                    ));\n330\t                }\n331\t                Err(e) => {\n332\t                    // Other error - log but proceed (best-effort caching)\n333\t                    tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"idempotency cache check failed, proceeding with write\");\n334\t                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"miss\");\n335\t                }\n336\t            }\n337\t        }\n338\t    }\n339\t\n340\t    // 1. Resolve alias to concrete index UID (plan §13.7)\n341\t    // Aliases are resolved before any processing; writes to multi-target aliases are rejected\n342\t    let index_uid = if state.config.aliases.enabled {\n343\t        // Check if the index is an alias\n344\t        let resolved = state.alias_registry.resolve(&index).await;\n345\t        if resolved != vec![index.clone()] {\n346\t            // It was an alias\n347\t            // Check if it's a multi-target alias (read-only, ILM-managed)\n348\t            if state.alias_registry.is_multi_target_alias(&index).await {\n349\t                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n350\t                    MiroirCode::MultiAliasNotWritable,\n351\t                    format!(\n352\t                        \"alias '{}' is a multi-target alias and is read-only (managed by ILM); writes must go to the concrete index or the write alias\",\n353\t                        index\n354\t                    ),\n355\t                ));\n356\t            }\n357\t            // Single-target alias: record resolution metric and use the target\n358\t            state.metrics.inc_alias_resolution(&index);\n359\t            resolved.into_iter().next().unwrap_or_else(|| index.clone())\n360\t        } else {\n361\t            // Not an alias, use the index name as-is\n362\t            index.clone()\n363\t        }\n364\t    } else {\n365\t        index.clone()\n366\t    };\n367\t\n368\t    // 1. Extract primary key from first document if not provided\n369\t    let primary_key = primary_key.or_else(|| {\n370\t        documents\n371\t            .first()\n372\t            .and_then(|doc| extract_primary_key(doc))\n373\t    });\n374\t\n375\t    let primary_key = primary_key.ok_or_else(|| {\n376\t        MeilisearchError::new(\n377\t            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n378\t            format!(\"primary key required for index `{}`\", index),\n379\t        )\n380\t    })?;\n381\t\n382\t    // 2. Validate all documents have the primary key and check for reserved field\n383\t    let anti_entropy_enabled = state.config.anti_entropy.enabled;\n384\t    let updated_at_field = &state.config.anti_entropy.updated_at_field;\n385\t    let ttl_enabled = state.config.ttl.enabled;\n386\t    let expires_at_field = &state.config.ttl.expires_at_field;\n387\t\n388\t    for (i, doc) in documents.iter().enumerate() {\n389\t        // Check for reserved field BEFORE checking primary key (per acceptance criteria)\n390\t        // _miroir_shard is ALWAYS reserved (plan §5)\n391\t        if doc.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_some() {\n392\t            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n393\t                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n394\t                \"document contains reserved field `_miroir_shard`\",\n395\t            ));\n396\t        }\n397\t\n398\t        // _miroir_updated_at is reserved ONLY when anti_entropy.enabled: true (plan §5, §13.8)\n399\t        if anti_entropy_enabled && doc.get(updated_at_field).is_some() {\n400\t            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n401\t                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n402\t                format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}` (reserved when anti_entropy.enabled: true)\", updated_at_field),\n403\t            ));\n404\t        }\n405\t\n406\t        // _miroir_expires_at is reserved ONLY when ttl.enabled: true (plan §5, §13.14)\n407\t        // When reserved, clients cannot SET it; the orchestrator controls it. When disabled,\n408\t        // client values pass through end-to-end.\n409\t        if ttl_enabled && doc.get(expires_at_field).is_some() {\n410\t            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n411\t                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n412\t                format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}` (reserved when ttl.enabled: true)\", expires_at_field),\n413\t            ));\n414\t        }\n415\t\n416\t        if doc.get(&primary_key).is_none() {\n417\t            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n418\t                MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n419\t                format!(\n420\t                    \"document at index {} missing primary key field `{}`\",\n421\t                    i, primary_key\n422\t                ),\n423\t            ));\n424\t        }\n425\t    }\n426\t\n427\t    // 3. Inject _miroir_shard and _miroir_updated_at into each document\n428\t    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n429\t    let shard_count = topology.shards;\n430\t    let rf = topology.rf();\n431\t    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n432\t\n433\t    // Get current timestamp in milliseconds since epoch for _miroir_updated_at stamping\n434\t    let now_ms = if anti_entropy_enabled {\n435\t        Some(\n436\t            std::time::SystemTime::now()\n437\t                .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n438\t                .unwrap_or_default()\n439\t                .as_millis() as u64\n440\t        )\n441\t    } else {\n442\t        None\n443\t    };\n444\t\n445\t    for doc in &mut documents {\n446\t        if let Some(pk_value) = doc.get(&primary_key).and_then(|v| v.as_str()) {\n447\t            let shard_id = shard_for_key(pk_value, shard_count);\n448\t            doc[\"_miroir_shard\"] = serde_json::json!(shard_id);\n449\t        }\n450\t\n451\t        // Stamp _miroir_updated_at when anti_entropy is enabled (plan §13.8)\n452\t        // This happens AFTER reserved field validation, so orchestrator-controlled injection is allowed\n453\t        if let Some(timestamp) = now_ms {\n454\t            doc[updated_at_field] = serde_json::json!(timestamp);\n455\t        }\n456\t    }\n457\t\n458\t    // 4. Group documents by target nodes (per-batch grouping for efficient fan-out)\n459\t    let node_documents = group_documents_by_shard(&documents, &primary_key, &topology)?;\n460\t\n461\t    // 5. Fan out to nodes and track quorum\n462\t    let client = HttpClient::new(\n463\t        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n464\t        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n465\t    );\n466\t\n467\t    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n468\t    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n469\t\n470\t    // For each shard, write to all RF nodes in each replica group (with dual-write support)\n471\t    for (shard_id, docs) in node_documents {\n472\t        // Get migration coordinator reference for dual-write detection\n473\t        let migration_coordinator = state.migration_coordinator.as_ref().map(|c| {\n474\t            // We need a read lock on the coordinator\n475\t            tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n476\t                tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(async {\n477\t                    c.read().await\n478\t                })\n479\t            })\n480\t        });\n481\t\n482\t        // Use migration-aware routing\n483\t        let targets = write_targets_with_migration(\n484\t            shard_id,\n485\t            &topology,\n486\t            migration_coordinator.as_deref(),\n487\t        );\n488\t\n489\t        if targets.is_empty() {\n490\t            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n491\t                MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n492\t                format!(\"no available nodes for shard {}\", shard_id),\n493\t            ));\n494\t        }\n495\t\n496\t        // Track which groups we're targeting for this shard\n497\t\n498\t        for node_id in targets {\n499\t            let node = topology\n500\t                .node(&node_id)\n501\t                .ok_or_else(|| MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable, \"node not found in topology\"))?;\n502\t\n503\t            let group_id = node.replica_group;\n504\t            quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node_id);\n505\t\n506\t            let req = WriteRequest {\n507\t                index_uid: index_uid.clone(),\n508\t                documents: docs.clone(),\n509\t                primary_key: Some(primary_key.clone()),\n510\t                origin: None, // Client write\n511\t            };\n512\t\n513\t            match client.write_documents(&node_id, &node.address, &req).await {\n514\t                Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n515\t                    quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node_id);\n516\t                    if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n517\t                        node_task_uids.insert(node_id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n518\t                    }\n519\t                }\n520\t                Ok(resp) => {\n521\t                    // Non-success response (validation error, etc.)\n522\t                    return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n523\t                }\n524\t                Err(_) => {\n525\t                    quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n526\t                }\n527\t            }\n528\t        }\n529\t    }\n530\t\n531\t    // 6. Apply two-rule quorum logic\n532\t    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n533\t    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n534\t\n535\t    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n536\t    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n537\t        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n538\t            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n539\t            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n540\t        ));\n541\t    }\n542\t\n543\t    // 6.5. Find the first group to reach quorum (for session pinning, plan §13.6)\n544\t    // Groups are checked in ascending order, so the first one with quorum is the first\n545\t    let first_quorum_group = (0..replica_group_count)\n546\t        .find(|&group_id| {\n547\t            let acks = *quorum_state.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n548\t            let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n549\t            acks >= quorum\n550\t        })\n551\t        .unwrap_or(0); // Default to group 0 if somehow no quorum (shouldn't happen here)\n552\t\n553\t    // 7. Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n554\t    let miroir_task = state\n555\t        .task_registry\n556\t        .register_with_metadata(\n557\t            node_task_uids.clone(),\n558\t            Some(index_uid.clone()),\n559\t            Some(\"documentAdditionOrUpdate\".to_string()),\n560\t        )\n561\t        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n562\t            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n563\t            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n564\t        ))?;\n565\t\n566\t    // 7.5. Record session pinning if session header present (plan §13.6)\n567\t    if let (Some(ref sid), true) = (&session_id, state.session_manager.is_enabled()) {\n568\t        if let Err(e) = state.session_manager.record_write_with_quorum(\n569\t            sid,\n570\t            miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n571\t            first_quorum_group,\n572\t        ).await {\n573\t            // Log error but don't fail the write - session pinning is best-effort\n574\t            tracing::error!(\n575\t                session_id = %sid,\n576\t                error = %e,\n577\t                \"failed to record session pinning for write\"\n578\t            );\n579\t        }\n580\t    }\n581\t\n582\t    // 7.6. Insert into idempotency cache if key was provided (plan §13.10)\n583\t    if state.config.idempotency.enabled {\n584\t        if let Some(ref key) = idempotency_key {\n585\t            use sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\n586\t            let body_hash = format!(\"{:x}\", Sha256::digest(serde_json::to_string(&documents).unwrap_or_default()));\n587\t            state.idempotency_cache.insert(\n588\t                key.clone(),\n589\t                body_hash,\n590\t                miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n591\t            ).await;\n592\t            // Update cache size metric\n593\t            state.metrics.set_idempotency_cache_size(state.idempotency_cache.size().await as u64);\n594\t        }\n595\t    }\n596\t\n597\t    // Build success response with degraded header and mtask ID\n598\t    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n599\t        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n600\t            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n601\t            indexUid: Some(index_uid.clone()),\n602\t            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n603\t            error: None,\n604\t            error_type: None,\n605\t            code: None,\n606\t            link: None,\n607\t        },\n608\t        degraded_groups,\n609\t    )\n610\t}\n611\t\n612\t/// Implementation for delete by IDs.\n613\tasync fn delete_by_ids_impl(\n614\t    index: String,\n615\t    req: DeleteByIdsRequest,\n616\t    state: &AppState,\n617\t    session_id: Option<String>,\n618\t    idempotency_key: Option<String>,\n619\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n620\t    if req.ids.is_empty() {\n621\t        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n622\t            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n623\t            \"cannot delete empty ID list\",\n624\t        ));\n625\t    }\n626\t\n627\t    // 0.5. Check idempotency cache (plan §13.10)\n628\t    if state.config.idempotency.enabled {\n629\t        if let Some(ref key) = idempotency_key {\n630\t            use sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\n631\t            let body_hash = format!(\"{:x}\", Sha256::digest(serde_json::to_string(&req).unwrap_or_default()));\n632\t\n633\t            match state.idempotency_cache.check(key, &body_hash).await {\n634\t                Ok(Some(cached_mtask_id)) => {\n635\t                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"dedup\");\n636\t                    return build_response_with_degraded_header(\n637\t                        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n638\t                            taskUid: Some(cached_mtask_id),\n639\t                            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n640\t                            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n641\t                            error: None,\n642\t                            error_type: None,\n643\t                            code: None,\n644\t                            link: None,\n645\t                        },\n646\t                        0,\n647\t                    );\n648\t                }\n649\t                Ok(None) => {\n650\t                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"miss\");\n651\t                }\n652\t                Err(miroir_core::error::MiroirError::IdempotencyKeyReused) => {\n653\t                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"conflict\");\n654\t                    return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n655\t                        MiroirCode::IdempotencyKeyReused,\n656\t                        \"idempotency key was already used with a different request body\",\n657\t                    ));\n658\t                }\n659\t                Err(e) => {\n660\t                    tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"idempotency cache check failed, proceeding with delete\");\n661\t                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"miss\");\n662\t                }\n663\t            }\n664\t        }\n665\t    }\n666\t\n667\t    // Resolve alias to concrete index UID (plan §13.7)\n668\t    let index_uid = if state.config.aliases.enabled {\n669\t        let resolved = state.alias_registry.resolve(&index).await;\n670\t        if resolved != vec![index.clone()] {\n671\t            // It was an alias - check if it's a multi-target alias (read-only)\n672\t            if state.alias_registry.is_multi_target_alias(&index).await {\n673\t                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n674\t                    MiroirCode::MultiAliasNotWritable,\n675\t                    format!(\n676\t                        \"alias '{}' is a multi-target alias and is read-only (managed by ILM); deletes must go to the concrete index or the write alias\",\n677\t                        index\n678\t                    ),\n679\t                ));\n680\t            }\n681\t            // Single-target alias: record resolution metric and use the target\n682\t            state.metrics.inc_alias_resolution(&index);\n683\t            resolved.into_iter().next().unwrap_or_else(|| index.clone())\n684\t        } else {\n685\t            index.clone()\n686\t        }\n687\t    } else {\n688\t        index.clone()\n689\t    };\n690\t\n691\t    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n692\t    let rf = topology.rf();\n693\t    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n694\t\n695\t    // Group IDs by target shard for independent per-shard routing\n696\t    let mut shard_ids: HashMap<u32, Vec<String>> = HashMap::new();\n697\t    for id in &req.ids {\n698\t        let shard_id = shard_for_key(id, topology.shards);\n699\t        shard_ids.entry(shard_id).or_default().push(id.clone());\n700\t    }\n701\t\n702\t    let client = HttpClient::new(\n703\t        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n704\t        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n705\t    );\n706\t\n707\t    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n708\t    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n709\t\n710\t    // For each shard, write to all RF nodes in each replica group\n711\t    for (shard_id, ids) in shard_ids {\n712\t        let targets = miroir_core::router::write_targets(shard_id, &topology);\n713\t\n714\t        if targets.is_empty() {\n715\t            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n716\t                MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n717\t                format!(\"no available nodes for shard {}\", shard_id),\n718\t            ));\n719\t        }\n720\t\n721\t        for node_id in targets {\n722\t            let node = topology\n723\t                .node(&node_id)\n724\t                .ok_or_else(|| MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable, \"node not found in topology\"))?;\n725\t\n726\t            let group_id = node.replica_group;\n727\t            quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node_id);\n728\t\n729\t            let delete_req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n730\t                index_uid: index_uid.clone(),\n731\t                ids: ids.clone(),\n732\t                origin: None, // Client write\n733\t            };\n734\t\n735\t            match client.delete_documents(&node_id, &node.address, &delete_req).await {\n736\t                Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n737\t                    quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node_id);\n738\t                    if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n739\t                        node_task_uids.insert(node_id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n740\t                    }\n741\t                }\n742\t                Ok(resp) => {\n743\t                    return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n744\t                }\n745\t                Err(_) => {\n746\t                    quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n747\t                }\n748\t            }\n749\t        }\n750\t    }\n751\t\n752\t    // Apply two-rule quorum logic\n753\t    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n754\t    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n755\t\n756\t    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n757\t    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n758\t        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n759\t            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n760\t            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n761\t        ));\n762\t    }\n763\t\n764\t    // Find the first group to reach quorum (for session pinning, plan §13.6)\n765\t    let first_quorum_group = (0..replica_group_count)\n766\t        .find(|&group_id| {\n767\t            let acks = *quorum_state.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n768\t            let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n769\t            acks >= quorum\n770\t        })\n771\t        .unwrap_or(0);\n772\t\n773\t    // Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n774\t    let miroir_task = state\n775\t        .task_registry\n776\t        .register_with_metadata(\n777\t            node_task_uids.clone(),\n778\t            Some(index.clone()),\n779\t            Some(\"documentDeletion\".to_string()),\n780\t        )\n781\t        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n782\t            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n783\t            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n784\t        ))?;\n785\t\n786\t    // Record session pinning if session header present (plan §13.6)\n787\t    if let (Some(ref sid), true) = (&session_id, state.session_manager.is_enabled()) {\n788\t        if let Err(e) = state.session_manager.record_write_with_quorum(\n789\t            sid,\n790\t            miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n791\t            first_quorum_group,\n792\t        ).await {\n793\t            tracing::error!(\n794\t                session_id = %sid,\n795\t                error = %e,\n796\t                \"failed to record session pinning for delete\"\n797\t            );\n798\t        }\n799\t    }\n800\t\n801\t    // Insert into idempotency cache if key was provided (plan §13.10)\n802\t    if state.config.idempotency.enabled {\n803\t        if let Some(ref key) = idempotency_key {\n804\t            use sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\n805\t            let body_hash = format!(\"{:x}\", Sha256::digest(serde_json::to_string(&req).unwrap_or_default()));\n806\t            state.idempotency_cache.insert(\n807\t                key.clone(),\n808\t                body_hash,\n809\t                miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n810\t            ).await;\n811\t            state.metrics.set_idempotency_cache_size(state.idempotency_cache.size().await as u64);\n812\t        }\n813\t    }\n814\t\n815\t    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n816\t        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n817\t            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n818\t            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n819\t            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n820\t            error: None,\n821\t            error_type: None,\n822\t            code: None,\n823\t            link: None,\n824\t        },\n825\t        degraded_groups,\n826\t    )\n827\t}\n828\t\n829\t/// Implementation for delete by filter (broadcast to all nodes).\n830\tasync fn delete_by_filter_impl(\n831\t    index: String,\n832\t    req: DeleteByFilterRequest,\n833\t    state: &AppState,\n834\t    session_id: Option<String>,\n835\t    idempotency_key: Option<String>,\n836\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n837\t    // 0.5. Check idempotency cache (plan §13.10)\n838\t    if state.config.idempotency.enabled {\n839\t        if let Some(ref key) = idempotency_key {\n840\t            use sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\n841\t            let body_hash = format!(\"{:x}\", Sha256::digest(serde_json::to_string(&req).unwrap_or_default()));\n842\t\n843\t            match state.idempotency_cache.check(key, &body_hash).await {\n844\t                Ok(Some(cached_mtask_id)) => {\n845\t                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"dedup\");\n846\t                    return build_response_with_degraded_header(\n847\t                        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n848\t                            taskUid: Some(cached_mtask_id),\n849\t                            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n850\t                            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n851\t                            error: None,\n852\t                            error_type: None,\n853\t                            code: None,\n854\t                            link: None,\n855\t                        },\n856\t                        0,\n857\t                    );\n858\t                }\n859\t                Ok(None) => {\n860\t                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"miss\");\n861\t                }\n862\t                Err(miroir_core::error::MiroirError::IdempotencyKeyReused) => {\n863\t                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"conflict\");\n864\t                    return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n865\t                        MiroirCode::IdempotencyKeyReused,\n866\t                        \"idempotency key was already used with a different request body\",\n867\t                    ));\n868\t                }\n869\t                Err(e) => {\n870\t                    tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"idempotency cache check failed, proceeding with delete by filter\");\n871\t                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"miss\");\n872\t                }\n873\t            }\n874\t        }\n875\t    }\n876\t\n877\t    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n878\t    let rf = topology.rf();\n879\t    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n880\t\n881\t    let client = HttpClient::new(\n882\t        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n883\t        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n884\t    );\n885\t\n886\t    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n887\t    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n888\t\n889\t    // Broadcast to all nodes (cannot shard-route for filters)\n890\t    for node in topology.nodes() {\n891\t        let group_id = node.replica_group;\n892\t        quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node.id);\n893\t\n894\t        match client\n895\t            .delete_documents_by_filter(&node.id, &node.address, &req)\n896\t            .await\n897\t        {\n898\t            Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n899\t                quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node.id);\n900\t                if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n901\t                    node_task_uids.insert(node.id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n902\t                }\n903\t            }\n904\t            Ok(resp) => {\n905\t                return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n906\t            }\n907\t            Err(_) => {\n908\t                quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n909\t            }\n910\t        }\n911\t    }\n912\t\n913\t    // Apply two-rule quorum logic\n914\t    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n915\t    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n916\t\n917\t    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n918\t    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n919\t        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n920\t            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n921\t            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n922\t        ));\n923\t    }\n924\t\n925\t    // Find the first group to reach quorum (for session pinning, plan §13.6)\n926\t    let first_quorum_group = (0..replica_group_count)\n927\t        .find(|&group_id| {\n928\t            let acks = *quorum_state.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n929\t            let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n930\t            acks >= quorum\n931\t        })\n932\t        .unwrap_or(0);\n933\t\n934\t    // Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n935\t    let miroir_task = state\n936\t        .task_registry\n937\t        .register_with_metadata(\n938\t            node_task_uids.clone(),\n939\t            Some(index.clone()),\n940\t            Some(\"documentDeletion\".to_string()),\n941\t        )\n942\t        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n943\t            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n944\t            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n945\t        ))?;\n946\t\n947\t    // Record session pinning if session header present (plan §13.6)\n948\t    if let (Some(ref sid), true) = (&session_id, state.session_manager.is_enabled()) {\n949\t        if let Err(e) = state.session_manager.record_write_with_quorum(\n950\t            sid,\n951\t            miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n952\t            first_quorum_group,\n953\t        ).await {\n954\t            tracing::error!(\n955\t                session_id = %sid,\n956\t                error = %e,\n957\t                \"failed to record session pinning for delete by filter\"\n958\t            );\n959\t        }\n960\t    }\n961\t\n962\t    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n963\t        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n964\t            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n965\t            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n966\t            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n967\t            error: None,\n968\t            error_type: None,\n969\t            code: None,\n970\t            link: None,\n971\t        },\n972\t        degraded_groups,\n973\t    )\n974\t}\n975\t\n976\t/// Extract primary key from a document by checking common field names.\n977\t///\n978\t/// Tries fields in order: id, pk, key, _id.\n979\tfn extract_primary_key(doc: &Value) -> Option<String> {\n980\t    [\"id\", \"pk\", \"key\", \"_id\"]\n981\t        .iter()\n982\t        .find(|&&key| doc.get(key).is_some())\n983\t        .map(|&s| s.to_string())\n984\t}\n985\t\n986\t/// Group documents by their target shard for fan-out optimization.\n987\t///\n988\t/// Returns a map of shard_id -> documents to send to that shard.\n989\t/// The caller then fans out each shard's documents to all RF nodes in each group.\n990\t///\n991\t/// This per-batch grouping minimizes HTTP fan-out count (critical at scale).\n992\tfn group_documents_by_shard(\n993\t    documents: &[Value],\n994\t    primary_key: &str,\n995\t    topology: &Topology,\n996\t) -> std::result::Result<HashMap<u32, Vec<Value>>, MeilisearchError> {\n997\t    let mut shard_documents: HashMap<u32, Vec<Value>> = HashMap::new();\n998\t\n999\t    for doc in documents {\n1000\t        let pk_value = doc\n1001\t            .get(primary_key)\n1002\t            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n1003\t            .ok_or_else(|| {\n1004\t                MeilisearchError::new(\n1005\t                    MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n1006\t                    \"primary key value must be a string\",\n1007\t                )\n1008\t            })?;\n1009\t\n1010\t        let shard_id = shard_for_key(pk_value, topology.shards);\n1011\t        shard_documents\n1012\t            .entry(shard_id)\n1013\t            .or_default()\n1014\t            .push(doc.clone());\n1015\t    }\n1016\t\n1017\t    Ok(shard_documents)\n1018\t}\n1019\t\n1020\t/// Build an error response from a node error.\n1021\tfn build_error_response(resp: WriteResponse) -> DocumentsWriteResponse {\n1022\t    DocumentsWriteResponse {\n1023\t        taskUid: resp.task_uid.map(|uid| uid.to_string()),\n1024\t        indexUid: None,\n1025\t        status: None,\n1026\t        error: resp.message,\n1027\t        error_type: resp.error_type,\n1028\t        code: resp.code,\n1029\t        link: None,\n1030\t    }\n1031\t}\n1032\t\n1033\t/// Build a success response with optional X-Miroir-Degraded header.\n1034\tfn build_response_with_degraded_header(\n1035\t    response: DocumentsWriteResponse,\n1036\t    degraded_groups: u32,\n1037\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n1038\t    let body = serde_json::to_string(&response).map_err(|e| {\n1039\t        MeilisearchError::new(\n1040\t            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n1041\t            format!(\"failed to serialize response: {}\", e),\n1042\t        )\n1043\t    })?;\n1044\t\n1045\t    let mut builder = Response::builder()\n1046\t        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n1047\t        .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"application/json\");\n1048\t\n1049\t    // Add X-Miroir-Degraded header if any groups were degraded\n1050\t    if degraded_groups > 0 {\n1051\t        builder = builder.header(HEADER_MIROIR_DEGRADED, format!(\"groups={}\", degraded_groups));\n1052\t    }\n1053\t\n1054\t    Ok(builder\n1055\t        .body(axum::body::Body::from(body))\n1056\t        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n1057\t            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n1058\t            format!(\"failed to build response: {}\", e),\n1059\t        ))?)\n1060\t}\n1061\t\n1062\t/// Build an error response as JSON (for forwarded node errors).\n1063\tfn build_json_error_response(resp: DocumentsWriteResponse) -> Response {\n1064\t    (\n1065\t        StatusCode::OK,\n1066\t        [(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"application/json\")],\n1067\t        Json(resp),\n1068\t    )\n1069\t        .into_response()\n1070\t}\n1071\t\n1072\t#[cfg(test)]\n1073\tmod tests {\n1074\t    use super::*;\n1075\t    use miroir_core::api_error::{MiroirCode, MeilisearchError};\n1076\t    use serde_json::json;\n1077\t\n1078\t    #[test]\n1079\t    fn test_extract_primary_key_common_fields() {\n1080\t        let doc_with_id = serde_json::json!({\"id\": \"test123\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n1081\t        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with_id), Some(\"id\".to_string()));\n1082\t\n1083\t        let doc_with_pk = serde_json::json!({\"pk\": \"test456\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n1084\t        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with_pk), Some(\"pk\".to_string()));\n1085\t\n1086\t        let doc_with_key = serde_json::json!({\"key\": \"test789\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n1087\t        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with_key), Some(\"key\".to_string()));\n1088\t\n1089\t        let #[test]\n1094\t    fn test_extract_primary_key_no_common_field() {\n1095\t        let doc = serde_json::json!({\"name\": \"Test\", \"value\": 42});\n1096\t        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc), None);\n1097\t    }\n1098\t\n1099\t    #[test]\n1100\t    fn test_extract_primary_key_priority() {\n1101\t        // Should return \"id\" first even if other fields exist\n1102\t        let doc = `_miroir_updated_at`: Reserved only when `anti_entropy.enabled: true`\n1111\t    // - `_miroir_expires_at`: Reserved only when `ttl.enabled: true`\n1112\t\n1113\t    /// Helper to build the expected reserved field error.\n1114\t    fn reserved_field_error(field: &str) -> MeilisearchError {\n1115\t        MeilisearchError::new(\n1116\t            MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n1117\t            format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}`\", field),\n1118\t        )\n1119\t    }\n1120\t\n1121\t    #[test]\n1122\t    fn test_reserved_field_miroir_shard_always_rejected() {\n1123\t        //    #[test]\n1143\t    fn test_reserved_field_miroir_expires_at_when_ttl_enabled() {\n1144\t        // When ttl.enabled: true, _miroir_expires_at is reserved\n1145\t        let field = #[test]\n1155\t    fn test_reserved_field_non_miroir_fields_allowed() {\n1156\t        // Non-reserved _miroir_ fields are allowed\n1157\t        let doc = json!({\"id\": \"test\", \"_miroir_custom\": }\n1161\t\n1162\t    /// Test matrix of all reserved field combinations per plan §5 table.\n1163\t    ///\n1164\t    /// Matrix cells (write behavior):\n1165\t    /// | Field           | Config disabled | Config enabled |\n1166\t    /// |-----------------|-----------------|----------------|\n1167\t    /// | _miroir_shard   | REJECTED (always)  | REJECTED (always) |\n1168\t    /// | _miroir_updated_at | ALLOWED      | REJECTED (anti_entropy) |\n1169\t    /// | _miroir_expires_at | ALLOWED      | REJECTED (ttl) |\n1170\t    #[test]\n1171\t    fn test_reserved_field_matrix() {\n1172\t        struct TestCase {\n1173\t            doc: Value,\n1174\t            description: &'static str,\n1175\t            has_shard: bool,\n1176\t            has_updated_at: bool,\n1177\t            has_expires_at: bool,\n1178\t        }\n1179\t\n1180\t        let test_cases = vec![\n1181\t            TestCase {\n1182\t                doc: json!({\"id\": \"test\"}),\n1183\t },\n1188\t            TestCase {\n1189\t                doc: json!({\"id\": \"test\", \"_miroir_shard\": 1}),\n1190\t                description: \"_miroir_shard always rejected\",\n1191\t                has_shard: true,\n1192\t                has_updated_at: false,\n1193\t                has_expires_at: false,\n1194\t            },\n1195\t            TestCase {\n1196\t                doc: has_shard: false,\n1199\t                has_updated_at: true,\n1200\t                has_expires_at: false,\n1201\t            },\n1202\t            TestCase {\n1203\t                doc: \"test\", \"_miroir_custom\": \"value\"}),\n1211\t                description: \"non-reserved _miroir_ fields allowed\",\n1212\t                has_shard: false,\n1213\t                has_updated_at: false,\n1214\t                has_expires_at: false,\n1215\t            },\n1216\t            TestCase {\n1217\t                doc: json!({\"id\": \"test\", \"_miroir_shard\": 1, \"_miroir_updated_at\": \"2024-01-01T00:00:00Z\"}),\n1218\t                description: \"multiple reserved fields present\",\n1219\t                has_shard: true,\n1220\t                has_updated_at: true,\n1221\t                has_expires_at: false,\n1222\t            },\n1223\t        ];\n1224\t\n1225\t        for tc in test_cases {\n1226\t            assert_eq!(tc.doc.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_some(), tc.has_shard, \"{}: shard check\", tc.description);\n1227\t            assert_eq!(tc.doc.get(\"_miroir_updated_at\").is_some(), tc.has_updated_at, \"{}: updated_at check\", tc.description);\n1228\t            assert_eq!(tc.doc.get(\"_miroir_expires_at\").is_some(), tc.has_expires_at, \"{}: expires_at check\", tc.description);\n1229\t        }\n1230\t    }\n1231\t\n1232\t    #[test]\n1233\t    fn test_reserved_field_error_format_matches_meilisearch_shape() {\n1234\t        let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n1235\t            MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n1236\t            \"document contains reserved field `_miroir_shard`\",\n1237\t        );\n1238\t\n1239\t        // Verify Meilisearch-compatible error shape: {message, code, type, link}\n1240\t        let json = serde_json::to_value(&err).unwrap();\n1241\t        assert_eq!(json[\"code\"], \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n1242\t        assert_eq!(json[\"type\"],
