Document write path: add, replace, and delete documents.\n//!\n//! Implements P2.2 write path:\n//! - Primary key extraction on the hot path\n//! - `_miroir_shard` injection\n//! - Reserved field rejection\n//! - Two-rule quorum\n//!\n//! Implements P2.5 task reconciliation:\n//! - Collects per-node task UIDs\n//! - Registers Miroir task ID (mtask-<uuid>)\n//! - Returns mtask ID to client\n//!\n//! Implements §13.6 session pinning:\n//! - Records writes with session header\n//! - Tracks pinned group (first to reach quorum)\n\nuse axum::extract::{Extension, Path, Query};\nuse axum::response::{IntoResponse, Response};\nuse axum::http::{StatusCode, header};\nuse axum::{Json, Router};\nuse miroir_core::api_error::{MiroirCode, MeilisearchError};\nuse miroir_core::router::{shard_for_key, write_targets_with_migration};\nuse miroir_core::scatter::{DeleteByIdsRequest, DeleteByFilterRequest, NodeClient, WriteRequest, WriteResponse};\nuse miroir_core::task::TaskRegistry;\nuse miroir_core::topology::{Topology, NodeId};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tracing::{debug, info, instrument};\n\nuse crate::client::HttpClient;\nuse crate::middleware::SessionId;\nuse crate::routes::admin_endpoints::AppState;\n\n/// Document write parameters from query string.\n#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]\npub struct DocumentsParams {\n    primaryKey: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Task response (Meilisearch-compatible).\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct TaskResponse {\n    taskUid: u64,\n    indexUid: String,\n    status: String,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    error: Option<Value>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n    error_type: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Response for write operations.\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct DocumentsWriteResponse {\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    taskUid: Option<String>, // Changed to String to hold mtask-<uuid>\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    indexUid: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    status: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    error: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n    error_type: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    code: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    link: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Header name for degraded write responses.\npub const HEADER_MIROIR_DEGRADED: &str = \"X-Miroir-Degraded\";\n\n/// Quorum tracking state for write operations.\n#[derive(Debug, Default)]\nstruct QuorumState {\n    /// Per-group ACK counts: group_id -> successful_ack_count\n    group_acks: HashMap<u32, usize>,\n    /// Per-group total node counts: group_id -> total_nodes_attempted\n    group_totals: HashMap<u32, usize>,\n    /// Groups that met quorum: group_id -> true\n    groups_met_quorum: HashMap<u32, bool>,\n    /// Total degraded groups count\n    degraded_groups: u32,\n}\n\nimpl QuorumState {\n    /// Record a write attempt to a node.\n    fn record_attempt(&mut self, group_id: u32, _node_id: &NodeId) {\n        *self.group_totals.entry(group_id).or_insert(0) += 1;\n    }\n\n    /// Record a successful write ACK from a node.\n    fn record_success(&mut self, group_id: u32, _node_id: &NodeId) {\n        *self.group_acks.entry(group_id).or_insert(0) += 1;\n    }\n\n    /// Record a failed write attempt from a node.\n    fn record_failure(&mut self, _group_id: u32) {\n        // Track that this group had a failure\n        // Degraded is determined after checking quorum\n    }\n\n    /// Check if a group has met quorum: floor(RF/2) + 1 ACKs required.\n    fn check_group_quorum(&mut self, group_id: u32, rf: usize) -> bool {\n        let acks = *self.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n        let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n        let met = acks >= quorum;\n        *self.groups_met_quorum.entry(group_id).or_insert(false) = met;\n        met\n    }\n\n    /// Count how many groups met quorum.\n    fn count_quorum_groups(&self) -> usize {\n        self.groups_met_quorum.values().filter(|&&v| v).count()\n    }\n\n    /// Count degraded groups (groups that exist but didn't meet quorum).\n    fn count_degraded_groups(&mut self, replica_group_count: u32, rf: usize) -> u32 {\n        let mut degraded = 0u32;\n        for group_id in 0..replica_group_count {\n            if !self.check_group_quorum(group_id, rf) {\n                // Only count as degraded if we attempted to write to this group\n                if self.group_totals.contains_key(&group_id) {\n                    degraded += 1;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        degraded\n    }\n}\n\n/// Build router for document endpoints.\npub fn router<S>() -> Router<S>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n{\n    Router::new()\n        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::post(post_documents))\n        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::put(put_documents))\n        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::delete(delete_documents))\n        .route(\"/:id\", axum::routing::delete(delete_document_by_id))\n}\n\n/// POST /indexes/{uid}/documents - Add documents.\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index))]\nasync fn post_documents(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Query(params): Query<DocumentsParams>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    session_id: Option<Extension<crate::middleware::SessionId>>,\n    headers: axum::http::HeaderMap,\n    Json(documents): Json<Vec<Value>>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    // Extract session ID from request extensions (set by session_pinning_middleware)\n    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n        let s = ext.0;\n        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n    });\n\n    // Extract idempotency key (plan §13.10)\n    let idempotency_key = headers\n        .get(\"Idempotency-Key\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .map(|s| s.to_string());\n\n    write_documents_impl(index, params.primaryKey, documents, &state, sid, idempotency_key).await\n}\n\n/// PUT /indexes/{uid}/documents - Replace documents.\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index))]\nasync fn put_documents(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Query(params): Query<DocumentsParams>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    session_id: Option<Extension<crate::middleware::SessionId>>,\n    headers: axum::http::HeaderMap,\n    Json(documents): Json<Vec<Value>>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n        let s = ext.0;\n        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n    });\n\n    // Extract idempotency key (plan §13.10)\n    let idempotency_key = headers\n        .get(\"Idempotency-Key\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .map(|s| s.to_string());\n\n    write_documents_impl(index, params.primaryKey, documents, &state, sid, idempotency_key).await\n}\n\n/// DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents - Delete by IDs or filter.\nasync fn delete_documents(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    session_id: Option<Extension<crate::middleware::SessionId>>,\n    headers: axum::http::HeaderMap,\n    Json(body): Json<Value>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n        let s = ext.0;\n        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n    });\n\n    // Extract idempotency key (plan §13.10)\n    let idempotency_key = headers\n        .get(\"Idempotency-Key\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .map(|s| s.to_string());\n\n    // Try to parse as delete by filter first\n    if let Some(filter) = body.get(\"filter\") {\n        let req = DeleteByFilterRequest {\n            index_uid: index.clone(),\n            filter: filter.clone(),\n            origin: None, // Client write\n        };\n        return delete_by_filter_impl(index, req, &state, sid, idempotency_key).await;\n    }\n\n    // Try to parse as delete by IDs\n    if let Some(ids) = body.get(\"ids\").and_then(|v| v.as_array()) {\n        let ids: Vec<String> = ids\n            .iter()\n            .filter_map(|v| v.as_str().map(|s| s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        if !ids.is_empty() {\n            let req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n                index_uid: index.clone(),\n                ids,\n                origin: None, // Client write\n            };\n            return delete_by_ids_impl(index, req, &state, sid, idempotency_key).await;\n        }\n    }\n\n    // If we get here, the request body is malformed\n    Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n        MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n        \"delete request must include either 'filter' or 'ids' field\",\n    ))\n}\n\n/// DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents/{id} - Delete single document by ID.\nasync fn delete_document_by_id(\n    Path((index, id)): Path<(String, String)>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    session_id: Option<Extension<crate::middleware::SessionId>>,\n    headers: axum::http::HeaderMap,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n        let s = ext.0;\n        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n    });\n\n    // Extract idempotency key (plan §13.10)\n    let idempotency_key = headers\n        .get(\"Idempotency-Key\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .map(|s| s.to_string());\n\n    let req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n        index_uid: index.clone(),\n        ids: vec![id],\n        origin: None, // Client write\n    };\n    delete_by_ids_impl(index, req, &state, sid, idempotency_key).await\n}\n\n/// Implementation for write documents (POST/PUT).\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index, session_id))]\nasync fn write_documents_impl(\n    index: String,\n    primary_key: Option<String>,\n    mut documents: Vec<Value>,\n    state: &AppState,\n    session_id: Option<String>,\n    idempotency_key: Option<String>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    if documents.is_empty() {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n            \"cannot write empty document batch\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // 0.5. Check idempotency cache (plan §13.10)\n    if state.config.idempotency.enabled {\n        if let Some(ref key) = idempotency_key {\n            // Compute SHA256 hash of the request body\n            use sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\n            let body_hash = format!(\"{:x}\", Sha256::digest(serde_json::to_string(&documents).unwrap_or_default()));\n\n            // Check cache\n            match state.idempotency_cache.check(key, &body_hash).await {\n                Ok(Some(cached_mtask_id)) => {\n                    // Idempotency hit: return cached mtask ID\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"dedup\");\n                    return build_response_with_degraded_header(\n                        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n                            taskUid: Some(cached_mtask_id),\n                            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n                            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n                            error: None,\n                            error_type: None,\n                            code: None,\n                            link: None,\n                        },\n                        0, // No degraded groups for cached response\n                    );\n                }\n                Ok(None) => {\n                    // Cache miss - proceed with processing\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"miss\");\n                }\n                Err(miroir_core::error::MiroirError::IdempotencyKeyReused) => {\n                    // Key exists but body hash differs\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"conflict\");\n                    return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                        MiroirCode::IdempotencyKeyReused,\n                        \"idempotency key was already used with a different request body\",\n                    ));\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    // Other error - log but proceed (best-effort caching)\n                    tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"idempotency cache check failed, proceeding with write\");\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"miss\");\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // 1. Resolve alias to concrete index UID (plan §13.7)\n    // Aliases are resolved before any processing; writes to multi-target aliases are rejected\n    let index_uid = if state.config.aliases.enabled {\n        // Check if the index is an alias\n        let resolved = state.alias_registry.resolve(&index).await;\n        if resolved != vec![index.clone()] {\n            // It was an alias\n            // Check if it's a multi-target alias (read-only, ILM-managed)\n            if state.alias_registry.is_multi_target_alias(&index).await {\n                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                    MiroirCode::MultiAliasNotWritable,\n                    format!(\n                        \"alias '{}' is a multi-target alias and is read-only (managed by ILM); writes must go to the concrete index or the write alias\",\n                        index\n                    ),\n                ));\n            }\n            // Single-target alias: record resolution metric and use the target\n            state.metrics.inc_alias_resolution(&index);\n            resolved.into_iter().next().unwrap_or_else(|| index.clone())\n        } else {\n            // Not an alias, use the index name as-is\n            index.clone()\n        }\n    } else {\n        index.clone()\n    };\n\n    // 1. Extract primary key from first document if not provided\n    let primary_key = primary_key.or_else(|| {\n        documents\n            .first()\n            .and_then(|doc| extract_primary_key(doc))\n    });\n\n    let primary_key = primary_key.ok_or_else(|| {\n        MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n            format!(\"primary key required for index `{}`\", index),\n        )\n    })?;\n\n    // 2. Validate all documents have the primary key and check for reserved field\n    let anti_entropy_enabled = state.config.anti_entropy.enabled;\n    let updated_at_field = &state.config.anti_entropy.updated_at_field;\n    let ttl_enabled = state.config.ttl.enabled;\n    let expires_at_field = &state.config.ttl.expires_at_field;\n\n    for (i, doc) in documents.iter().enumerate() {\n        // Check for reserved field BEFORE checking primary key (per acceptance criteria)\n        // _miroir_shard is ALWAYS reserved (plan §5)\n        if doc.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_some() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n                \"document contains reserved field `_miroir_shard`\",\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // _miroir_updated_at is reserved ONLY when anti_entropy.enabled: true (plan §5, §13.8)\n        if anti_entropy_enabled && doc.get(updated_at_field).is_some() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n                format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}` (reserved when anti_entropy.enabled: true)\", updated_at_field),\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // _miroir_expires_at is reserved ONLY when ttl.enabled: true (plan §5, §13.14)\n        // When reserved, clients cannot SET it; the orchestrator controls it. When disabled,\n        // client values pass through end-to-end.\n        if ttl_enabled && doc.get(expires_at_field).is_some() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n                format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}` (reserved when ttl.enabled: true)\", expires_at_field),\n            ));\n        }\n\n        if doc.get(&primary_key).is_none() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n                format!(\n                    \"document at index {} missing primary key field `{}`\",\n                    i, primary_key\n                ),\n            ));\n        }\n    }\n\n    // 3. Inject _miroir_shard and _miroir_updated_at into each document\n    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n    let shard_count = topology.shards;\n    let rf = topology.rf();\n    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n\n    // Get current timestamp in milliseconds since epoch for _miroir_updated_at stamping\n    let now_ms = if anti_entropy_enabled {\n        Some(\n            std::time::SystemTime::now()\n                .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n                .unwrap_or_default()\n                .as_millis() as u64\n        )\n    } else {\n        None\n    };\n\n    for doc in &mut documents {\n        if let Some(pk_value) = doc.get(&primary_key).and_then(|v| v.as_str()) {\n            let shard_id = shard_for_key(pk_value, shard_count);\n            doc[\"_miroir_shard\"] = serde_json::json!(shard_id);\n        }\n\n        // Stamp _miroir_updated_at when anti_entropy is enabled (plan §13.8)\n        // This happens AFTER reserved field validation, so orchestrator-controlled injection is allowed\n        if let Some(timestamp) = now_ms {\n            doc[updated_at_field] = serde_json::json!(timestamp);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // 4. Group documents by target nodes (per-batch grouping for efficient fan-out)\n    let node_documents = group_documents_by_shard(&documents, &primary_key, &topology)?;\n\n    // 5. Fan out to nodes and track quorum\n    let client = HttpClient::new(\n        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n    );\n\n    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n\n    // For each shard, write to all RF nodes in each replica group (with dual-write support)\n    for (shard_id, docs) in node_documents {\n        // Get migration coordinator reference for dual-write detection\n        let migration_coordinator = state.migration_coordinator.as_ref().map(|c| {\n            // We need a read lock on the coordinator\n            tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n                tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(async {\n                    c.read().await\n                })\n            })\n        });\n\n        // Use migration-aware routing\n        let targets = write_targets_with_migration(\n            shard_id,\n            &topology,\n            migration_coordinator.as_deref(),\n        );\n\n        if targets.is_empty() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n                format!(\"no available nodes for shard {}\", shard_id),\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // Track which groups we're targeting for this shard\n\n        for node_id in targets {\n            let node = topology\n                .node(&node_id)\n                .ok_or_else(|| MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable, \"node not found in topology\"))?;\n\n            let group_id = node.replica_group;\n            quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node_id);\n\n            let req = WriteRequest {\n                index_uid: index_uid.clone(),\n                documents: docs.clone(),\n                primary_key: Some(primary_key.clone()),\n                origin: None, // Client write\n            };\n\n            match client.write_documents(&node_id, &node.address, &req).await {\n                Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n                    quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node_id);\n                    if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n                        node_task_uids.insert(node_id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n                    }\n                }\n                Ok(resp) => {\n                    // Non-success response (validation error, etc.)\n                    return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n                }\n                Err(_) => {\n                    quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // 6. Apply two-rule quorum logic\n    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n\n    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // 6.5. Find the first group to reach quorum (for session pinning, plan §13.6)\n    // Groups are checked in ascending order, so the first one with quorum is the first\n    let first_quorum_group = (0..replica_group_count)\n        .find(|&group_id| {\n            let acks = *quorum_state.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n            let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n            acks >= quorum\n        })\n        .unwrap_or(0); // Default to group 0 if somehow no quorum (shouldn't happen here)\n\n    // 7. Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n    let miroir_task = state\n        .task_registry\n        .register_with_metadata(\n            node_task_uids.clone(),\n            Some(index_uid.clone()),\n            Some(\"documentAdditionOrUpdate\".to_string()),\n        )\n        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n        ))?;\n\n    // 7.5. Record session pinning if session header present (plan §13.6)\n    if let (Some(ref sid), true) = (&session_id, state.session_manager.is_enabled()) {\n        if let Err(e) = state.session_manager.record_write_with_quorum(\n            sid,\n            miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n            first_quorum_group,\n        ).await {\n            // Log error but don't fail the write - session pinning is best-effort\n            tracing::error!(\n                session_id = %sid,\n                error = %e,\n                \"failed to record session pinning for write\"\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    // 7.6. Insert into idempotency cache if key was provided (plan §13.10)\n    if state.config.idempotency.enabled {\n        if let Some(ref key) = idempotency_key {\n            use sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\n            let body_hash = format!(\"{:x}\", Sha256::digest(serde_json::to_string(&documents).unwrap_or_default()));\n            state.idempotency_cache.insert(\n                key.clone(),\n                body_hash,\n                miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n            ).await;\n            // Update cache size metric\n            state.metrics.set_idempotency_cache_size(state.idempotency_cache.size().await as u64);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Build success response with degraded header and mtask ID\n    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n            indexUid: Some(index_uid.clone()),\n            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n            error: None,\n            error_type: None,\n            code: None,\n            link: None,\n        },\n        degraded_groups,\n    )\n}\n\n/// Implementation for delete by IDs.\nasync fn delete_by_ids_impl(\n    index: String,\n    req: DeleteByIdsRequest,\n    state: &AppState,\n    session_id: Option<String>,\n    idempotency_key: Option<String>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    if req.ids.is_empty() {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n            \"cannot delete empty ID list\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // 0.5. Check idempotency cache (plan §13.10)\n    if state.config.idempotency.enabled {\n        if let Some(ref key) = idempotency_key {\n            use sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\n            let body_hash = format!(\"{:x}\", Sha256::digest(serde_json::to_string(&req).unwrap_or_default()));\n\n            match state.idempotency_cache.check(key, &body_hash).await {\n                Ok(Some(cached_mtask_id)) => {\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"dedup\");\n                    return build_response_with_degraded_header(\n                        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n                            taskUid: Some(cached_mtask_id),\n                            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n                            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n                            error: None,\n                            error_type: None,\n                            code: None,\n                            link: None,\n                        },\n                        0,\n                    );\n                }\n                Ok(None) => {\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"miss\");\n                }\n                Err(miroir_core::error::MiroirError::IdempotencyKeyReused) => {\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"conflict\");\n                    return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                        MiroirCode::IdempotencyKeyReused,\n                        \"idempotency key was already used with a different request body\",\n                    ));\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"idempotency cache check failed, proceeding with delete\");\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"miss\");\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Resolve alias to concrete index UID (plan §13.7)\n    let index_uid = if state.config.aliases.enabled {\n        let resolved = state.alias_registry.resolve(&index).await;\n        if resolved != vec![index.clone()] {\n            // It was an alias - check if it's a multi-target alias (read-only)\n            if state.alias_registry.is_multi_target_alias(&index).await {\n                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                    MiroirCode::MultiAliasNotWritable,\n                    format!(\n                        \"alias '{}' is a multi-target alias and is read-only (managed by ILM); deletes must go to the concrete index or the write alias\",\n                        index\n                    ),\n                ));\n            }\n            // Single-target alias: record resolution metric and use the target\n            state.metrics.inc_alias_resolution(&index);\n            resolved.into_iter().next().unwrap_or_else(|| index.clone())\n        } else {\n            index.clone()\n        }\n    } else {\n        index.clone()\n    };\n\n    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n    let rf = topology.rf();\n    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n\n    // Group IDs by target shard for independent per-shard routing\n    let mut shard_ids: HashMap<u32, Vec<String>> = HashMap::new();\n    for id in &req.ids {\n        let shard_id = shard_for_key(id, topology.shards);\n        shard_ids.entry(shard_id).or_default().push(id.clone());\n    }\n\n    let client = HttpClient::new(\n        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n    );\n\n    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n\n    // For each shard, write to all RF nodes in each replica group\n    for (shard_id, ids) in shard_ids {\n        let targets = miroir_core::router::write_targets(shard_id, &topology);\n\n        if targets.is_empty() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n                format!(\"no available nodes for shard {}\", shard_id),\n            ));\n        }\n\n        for node_id in targets {\n            let node = topology\n                .node(&node_id)\n                .ok_or_else(|| MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable, \"node not found in topology\"))?;\n\n            let group_id = node.replica_group;\n            quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node_id);\n\n            let delete_req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n                index_uid: index_uid.clone(),\n                ids: ids.clone(),\n                origin: None, // Client write\n            };\n\n            match client.delete_documents(&node_id, &node.address, &delete_req).await {\n                Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n                    quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node_id);\n                    if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n                        node_task_uids.insert(node_id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n                    }\n                }\n                Ok(resp) => {\n                    return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n                }\n                Err(_) => {\n                    quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Apply two-rule quorum logic\n    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n\n    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Find the first group to reach quorum (for session pinning, plan §13.6)\n    let first_quorum_group = (0..replica_group_count)\n        .find(|&group_id| {\n            let acks = *quorum_state.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n            let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n            acks >= quorum\n        })\n        .unwrap_or(0);\n\n    // Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n    let miroir_task = state\n        .task_registry\n        .register_with_metadata(\n            node_task_uids.clone(),\n            Some(index.clone()),\n            Some(\"documentDeletion\".to_string()),\n        )\n        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n        ))?;\n\n    // Record session pinning if session header present (plan §13.6)\n    if let (Some(ref sid), true) = (&session_id, state.session_manager.is_enabled()) {\n        if let Err(e) = state.session_manager.record_write_with_quorum(\n            sid,\n            miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n            first_quorum_group,\n        ).await {\n            tracing::error!(\n                session_id = %sid,\n                error = %e,\n                \"failed to record session pinning for delete\"\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Insert into idempotency cache if key was provided (plan §13.10)\n    if state.config.idempotency.enabled {\n        if let Some(ref key) = idempotency_key {\n            use sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\n            let body_hash = format!(\"{:x}\", Sha256::digest(serde_json::to_string(&req).unwrap_or_default()));\n            state.idempotency_cache.insert(\n                key.clone(),\n                body_hash,\n                miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n            ).await;\n            state.metrics.set_idempotency_cache_size(state.idempotency_cache.size().await as u64);\n        }\n    }\n\n    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n            error: None,\n            error_type: None,\n            code: None,\n            link: None,\n        },\n        degraded_groups,\n    )\n}\n\n/// Implementation for delete by filter (broadcast to all nodes).\nasync fn delete_by_filter_impl(\n    index: String,\n    req: DeleteByFilterRequest,\n    state: &AppState,\n    session_id: Option<String>,\n    idempotency_key: Option<String>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    // 0.5. Check idempotency cache (plan §13.10)\n    if state.config.idempotency.enabled {\n        if let Some(ref key) = idempotency_key {\n            use sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\n            let body_hash = format!(\"{:x}\", Sha256::digest(serde_json::to_string(&req).unwrap_or_default()));\n\n            match state.idempotency_cache.check(key, &body_hash).await {\n                Ok(Some(cached_mtask_id)) => {\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"dedup\");\n                    return build_response_with_degraded_header(\n                        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n                            taskUid: Some(cached_mtask_id),\n                            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n                            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n                            error: None,\n                            error_type: None,\n                            code: None,\n                            link: None,\n                        },\n                        0,\n                    );\n                }\n                Ok(None) => {\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"miss\");\n                }\n                Err(miroir_core::error::MiroirError::IdempotencyKeyReused) => {\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"conflict\");\n                    return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                        MiroirCode::IdempotencyKeyReused,\n                        \"idempotency key was already used with a different request body\",\n                    ));\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"idempotency cache check failed, proceeding with delete by filter\");\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"miss\");\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n    let rf = topology.rf();\n    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n\n    let client = HttpClient::new(\n        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n    );\n\n    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n\n    // Broadcast to all nodes (cannot shard-route for filters)\n    for node in topology.nodes() {\n        let group_id = node.replica_group;\n        quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node.id);\n\n        match client\n            .delete_documents_by_filter(&node.id, &node.address, &req)\n            .await\n        {\n            Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n                quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node.id);\n                if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n                    node_task_uids.insert(node.id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n                }\n            }\n            Ok(resp) => {\n                return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n            }\n            Err(_) => {\n                quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Apply two-rule quorum logic\n    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n\n    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Find the first group to reach quorum (for session pinning, plan §13.6)\n    let first_quorum_group = (0..replica_group_count)\n        .find(|&group_id| {\n            let acks = *quorum_state.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n            let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n            acks >= quorum\n        })\n        .unwrap_or(0);\n\n    // Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n    let miroir_task = state\n        .task_registry\n        .register_with_metadata(\n            node_task_uids.clone(),\n            Some(index.clone()),\n            Some(\"documentDeletion\".to_string()),\n        )\n        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n        ))?;\n\n    // Record session pinning if session header present (plan §13.6)\n    if let (Some(ref sid), true) = (&session_id, state.session_manager.is_enabled()) {\n        if let Err(e) = state.session_manager.record_write_with_quorum(\n            sid,\n            miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n            first_quorum_group,\n        ).await {\n            tracing::error!(\n                session_id = %sid,\n                error = %e,\n                \"failed to record session pinning for delete by filter\"\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n            error: None,\n            error_type: None,\n            code: None,\n            link: None,\n        },\n        degraded_groups,\n    )\n}\n\n/// Extract primary key from a document by checking common field names.\n///\n/// Tries fields in order: id, pk, key, _id.\nfn extract_primary_key(doc: &Value) -> Option<String> {\n    [\"id\", \"pk\", \"key\", \"_id\"]\n        .iter()\n        .find(|&&key| doc.get(key).is_some())\n        .map(|&s| s.to_string())\n}\n\n/// Group documents by their target shard for fan-out optimization.\n///\n/// Returns a map of shard_id -> documents to send to that shard.\n/// The caller then fans out each shard's documents to all RF nodes in each group.\n///\n/// This per-batch grouping minimizes HTTP fan-out count (critical at scale).\nfn group_documents_by_shard(\n    documents: &[Value],\n    primary_key: &str,\n    topology: &Topology,\n) -> std::result::Result<HashMap<u32, Vec<Value>>, MeilisearchError> {\n    let mut shard_documents: HashMap<u32, Vec<Value>> = HashMap::new();\n\n    for doc in documents {\n        let pk_value = doc\n            .get(primary_key)\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n            .ok_or_else(|| {\n                MeilisearchError::new(\n                    MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n                    \"primary key value must be a string\",\n                )\n            })?;\n\n        let shard_id = shard_for_key(pk_value, topology.shards);\n        shard_documents\n            .entry(shard_id)\n            .or_default()\n            .push(doc.clone());\n    }\n\n    Ok(shard_documents)\n}\n\n/// Build an error response from a node error.\nfn build_error_response(resp: WriteResponse) -> DocumentsWriteResponse {\n    DocumentsWriteResponse {\n        taskUid: resp.task_uid.map(|uid| uid.to_string()),\n        indexUid: None,\n        status: None,\n        error: resp.message,\n        error_type: resp.error_type,\n        code: resp.code,\n        link: None,\n    }\n}\n\n/// Build a success response with optional X-Miroir-Degraded header.\nfn build_response_with_degraded_header(\n    response: DocumentsWriteResponse,\n    degraded_groups: u32,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    let body = serde_json::to_string(&response).map_err(|e| {\n        MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to serialize response: {}\", e),\n        )\n    })?;\n\n    let mut builder = Response::builder()\n        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n        .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"application/json\");\n\n    // Add X-Miroir-Degraded header if any groups were degraded\n    if degraded_groups > 0 {\n        builder = builder.header(HEADER_MIROIR_DEGRADED, format!(\"groups={}\", degraded_groups));\n    }\n\n    Ok(builder\n        .body(axum::body::Body::from(body))\n        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to build response: {}\", e),\n        ))?)\n}\n\n/// Build an error response as JSON (for forwarded node errors).\nfn build_json_error_response(resp: DocumentsWriteResponse) -> Response {\n    (\n        StatusCode::OK,\n        [(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"application/json\")],\n        Json(resp),\n    )\n        .into_response()\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use miroir_core::api_error::{MiroirCode, MeilisearchError};\n    use serde_json::json;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_extract_primary_key_common_fields() {\n        let doc_with_id = serde_json::json!({\"id\": \"test123\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with_id), Some(\"id\".to_string()));\n\n        let doc_with_pk = serde_json::json!({\"pk\": \"test456\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with_pk), Some(\"pk\".to_string()));\n\n        let doc_with_key = serde_json::json!({\"key\": \"test789\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with_key), Some(\"key\".to_string()));\n\n        let doc_with__id = serde_json::json!({\"_id\": \"test000\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with__id), Some(\"_id\".to_string()));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_extract_primary_key_no_common_field() {\n        let doc = serde_json::json!({\"name\": \"Test\", \"value\": 42});\n        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc), None);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_extract_primary_key_priority() {\n        // Should return \"id\" first even if other fields exist\n        let doc = serde_json::json!({\"id\": \"test\", \"pk\": \"other\", \"key\": \"another\"});\n        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc), Some(\"id\".to_string()));\n    }\n\n    // Reserved field validation tests (P2.9)\n    //\n    // Tests the reserved field matrix per plan §5:\n    // - `_miroir_shard`: Always reserved (unconditional)\n    // - `_miroir_updated_at`: Reserved only when `anti_entropy.enabled: true`\n    // - `_miroir_expires_at`: Reserved only when `ttl.enabled: true`\n\n    /// Helper to build the expected reserved field error.\n    fn reserved_field_error(field: &str) -> MeilisearchError {\n        MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n            format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}`\", field),\n        )\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_miroir_shard_always_rejected() {\n        // _miroir_shard is ALWAYS reserved regardless of config\n        let err = reserved_field_error(\"_miroir_shard\");\n        assert_eq!(err.code, \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n        assert_eq!(err.http_status(), 400);\n        assert_eq!(err.error_type, miroir_core::api_error::ErrorType::InvalidRequest);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_miroir_updated_at_when_anti_entropy_enabled() {\n        // When anti_entropy.enabled: true, _miroir_updated_at is reserved\n        let field = \"_miroir_updated_at\";\n        let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n            format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}` (reserved when anti_entropy.enabled: true)\", field),\n        );\n        assert_eq!(err.code, \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n        assert_eq!(err.http_status(), 400);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_miroir_expires_at_when_ttl_enabled() {\n        // When ttl.enabled: true, _miroir_expires_at is reserved\n        let field = \"_miroir_expires_at\";\n        let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n            format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}` (reserved when ttl.enabled: true)\", field),\n        );\n        assert_eq!(err.code, \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n        assert_eq!(err.http_status(), 400);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_non_miroir_fields_allowed() {\n        // Non-reserved _miroir_ fields are allowed\n        let doc = json!({\"id\": \"test\", \"_miroir_custom\": \"value\", \"_miroir_metadata\": {\"key\": \"val\"}});\n        assert!(doc.get(\"_miroir_custom\").is_some());\n        assert!(doc.get(\"_miroir_metadata\").is_some());\n    }\n\n    /// Test matrix of all reserved field combinations per plan §5 table.\n    ///\n    /// Matrix cells (write behavior):\n    /// | Field           | Config disabled | Config enabled |\n    /// |-----------------|-----------------|----------------|\n    /// | _miroir_shard   | REJECTED (always)  | REJECTED (always) |\n    /// | _miroir_updated_at | ALLOWED      | REJECTED (anti_entropy) |\n    /// | _miroir_expires_at | ALLOWED      | REJECTED (ttl) |\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_matrix() {\n        struct TestCase {\n            doc: Value,\n            description: &'static str,\n            has_shard: bool,\n            has_updated_at: bool,\n            has_expires_at: bool,\n        }\n\n        let test_cases = vec![\n            TestCase {\n                doc: json!({\"id\": \"test\"}),\n                description: \"clean document should pass\",\n                has_shard: false,\n                has_updated_at: false,\n                has_expires_at: false,\n            },\n            TestCase {\n                doc: json!({\"id\": \"test\", \"_miroir_shard\": 1}),\n                description: \"_miroir_shard always rejected\",\n                has_shard: true,\n                has_updated_at: false,\n                has_expires_at: false,\n            },\n            TestCase {\n                doc: json!({\"id\": \"test\", \"_miroir_updated_at\": \"2024-01-01T00:00:00Z\"}),\n                description: \"_miroir_updated_at allowed when anti_entropy disabled\",\n                has_shard: false,\n                has_updated_at: true,\n                has_expires_at: false,\n            },\n            TestCase {\n                doc: json!({\"id\": \"test\", \"_miroir_expires_at\": \"2024-12-31T23:59:59Z\"}),\n                description: \"_miroir_expires_at allowed when ttl.disabled\",\n                has_shard: false,\n                has_updated_at: false,\n                has_expires_at: true,\n            },\n            TestCase {\n                doc: json!({\"id\": \"test\", \"_miroir_custom\": \"value\"}),\n                description: \"non-reserved _miroir_ fields allowed\",\n                has_shard: false,\n                has_updated_at: false,\n                has_expires_at: false,\n            },\n            TestCase {\n                doc: json!({\"id\": \"test\", \"_miroir_shard\": 1, \"_miroir_updated_at\": \"2024-01-01T00:00:00Z\"}),\n                description: \"multiple reserved fields present\",\n                has_shard: true,\n                has_updated_at: true,\n                has_expires_at: false,\n            },\n        ];\n\n        for tc in test_cases {\n            assert_eq!(tc.doc.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_some(), tc.has_shard, \"{}: shard check\", tc.description);\n            assert_eq!(tc.doc.get(\"_miroir_updated_at\").is_some(), tc.has_updated_at, \"{}: updated_at check\", tc.description);\n            assert_eq!(tc.doc.get(\"_miroir_expires_at\").is_some(), tc.has_expires_at, \"{}: expires_at check\", tc.description);\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_error_format_matches_meilisearch_shape() {\n        let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n            \"document contains reserved field `_miroir_shard`\",\n        );\n\n        // Verify Meilisearch-compatible error shape: {message, code, type, link}\n        let json = serde_json::to_value(&err).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(json[\"code\"], \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n        assert_eq!(json[\"type\"], \"invalid_request\");\n        assert!(json[\"message\"].is_string());\n        assert!(json[\"link\"].as_str().unwrap().contains(\"miroir_reserved_field\"));\n        assert_eq!(json[\"message\"], \"document contains reserved field `_miroir_shard`\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_error_all_fields_present() {\n        let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n assert!(err.link.unwrap().contains(\"miroir_reserved_field\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_orchestrator_shard_injection_flow() {\n        // Simulate the orchestrator injection flow:\n        // 1. Client sends document WITHOUT _miroir_shard\n        // 2. Validation passes (no _miroir_shard present)\n        // 3. Orchestrator injects _miroir_shard for routing\n        // 4. Write proceeds with injected field\n\n        let client_doc = json!({\"id\": \"user:123\", \"name\": \"Test User\"});\n        assert!(client_doc.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_none(), \"client doc should not have _miroir_shard\");\n\n        // Simulate orchestrator injection (happens AFTER validation at line 279-290)\n        let mut doc_with_shard = client_doc.clone();\n        doc_with_shard[\"_miroir_shard\"] = json!(5);\n\n        assert_eq!(doc_with_shard[\"_miroir_shard\"], 5, \"orchestrator should inject _miroir_shard\");\n        assert!(doc_with_shard.get(\"id\").is_some(), \"primary key should still be present\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_validation_order() {\n        // _miroir_shard is checked BEFORE primary key validation (per acceptance criteria)\n        // This ensures clients can't bypass validation by including both fields\n\n        let doc_with_shard_no_pk = json!({\"_miroir_shard\": 1, \"name\": \"No PK\"});\n        assert!(doc_with_shard_no_pk.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_some());\n        assert!(doc_with_shard_no_pk.get(\"id\").is_none());\n\n        // The validation should catch _miroir_shard first, not missing primary key\n        let err = reserved_field_error(\"_miroir_shard\");\n        assert_eq!(err.code, \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n    }\n\n    // P2.9: Complete reserved field matrix tests\n    //\n    // Matrix cells per plan §5:\n    // | Field           | Config disabled | Config enabled |\n    // the stamping logic (plan §13.8)\n\n        let client_doc = json!({\"id\": \"user:123\", \"name\": \"Test User\"});\n        assert!(client_doc.get(\"_miroir_updated_at\").is_none(), \"client doc present\");\n    }\n}\n","numLines":1376,"startLine":1,"totalLines":1376}}}

   verify_before_swap: true,\n            retain_old_index_hours: 48,\n            allowed_windows: Vec::new(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// OAuthProxyConfig::default(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(default)]\npub struct OAuthProxyConfig {\n    pub user_header: String,\n    pub groups_header: String,\n    pub filter_template: Option<String>,\n    pub attribute_map: HashMap<String, String>,\n}\n\nimpl Default for OAuthProxyConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            user_header: \"X-Forwarded-User\".into(),\n            groups_header: \"X-Forwarded-Groups\".into(),\n            filter_template: Some(\"tenant IN [{groups}]\".into()),\n            attribute_map: {\n                let mut m = HashMap::new();\n                m.insert(\"groups\".into(), \"groups_array\".into());\n                m.insert(\"user\".into(), \"user_id_string\".into());\n                m\n            },\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(default)]\npub struct SearchUiRateLimitConfig sink: \"cdc\".into(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// 13.22  Rebalancer (P4.1 background worker)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Rebalancer configuration (plan §4 Phase 4.1).\n///\n/// The rebalancer is a background Tokio task that orchestrates shard migration\n/// during topology changes (node add/drain/fail/recover). A slow node shows up as an outlier span in Tempo.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(default)]\npub struct TracingConfig {\n    /// Enable or disable OTel tracing. coordinator.\n153\t/// 2. **Search phase**: Send the search query with global IDF attached so that\n154\t///    scoring uses corpus-wide statistics instead of per-shard local IDF.\n155\t///\n156\t/// Returns `X-Miroir-Degraded: shards=X,Y,Z` header when any shards are unavailable.\n157\t/// Strips `_miroir_shard` from all hits; strips `_rankingScore` unless client\n158\t/// explicitly requested it.\n159\t///\n160\t/// Session pinning (plan §13.6): If `X-Miroir-Session` header is present and\n161\t/// the session has a pending write, routes to the pinned group for read-your-writes.\n162\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index))]\n163\tasync fn search_handler(\n164\t    Path(index): Path<String>,\n165\t    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n166\t    headers: HeaderMap,\n167\t    session_id: Option<Extension<SessionId>>,\n168\t    Json(body): Json<SearchRequestBody>,\n169\t) -> Result<Response, StatusCode> {\n170\t    let start = Instant::now();\n171\t    let client_requested_score = body.ranking_score.unwrap_or(false);\n172\t\n173\t    // Extract session ID from request extensions (set by session_pinning_middleware)\n174\t    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n175\t        let s = ext.0;\n176\t        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n177\t    });\n178\t\n179\t    // Extract source IP from X-Forwarded-For or X-Real-IP (trust proxy)\n180\t    let source_ip = headers\n181\t        .get(\"x-forwarded-for\")\n182\t        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n183\t        .and_then(|s| s.split(',').next())\n184\t        .or_else(|| headers.get(\"x-real-ip\").and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok()))\n185\t        .unwrap_or(\"unknown\")\n186\t        .trim()\n187\t        .to_string();\n188\t\n189\t    // Check rate limit for search UI (plan §4)\n190\t    let (limit, window_seconds) = match parse_rate_limit(&state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip) {\n191\t        Ok(parsed) => parsed,\n192\t        Err(e) => {\n193\t            warn!(error = %e, \"invalid search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip config, using default\");\n194\t            (60, 60) // Default: 60 requests per minute\n195\t        }\n196\t    };\n197\t\n198\t    let backend = state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.backend.as_str();\n199\t    if backend == \"redis\" {\n200\t        if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n201\t            match redis.check_rate_limit_search_ui(&source_ip, limit, window_seconds) {\n202\t                Ok((allowed, _wait_seconds)) => {\n203\t                    if !allowed {\n204\t                        warn!(\n205\t                            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n206\t                            \"search UI rate limited (redis)\"\n207\t                        );\n208\t                        return Err(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS);\n209\t                    }\n210\t                    // Allowed, proceed\n211\t                }\n212\t                Err(e) => {\n213\t                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to check search UI rate limit\");\n214\t                    // Continue anyway on error (fail-open)\n215\t                }\n216\t            }\n217\t        }\n218\t    } else if backend == \"local\" {\n219\t        let (allowed, _wait_seconds) = state.local_search_ui_rate_limiter.check(\n220\t            &source_ip,\n221\t            limit,\n222\t            window_seconds * 1000,\n223\t        );\n224\t        if !allowed {\n225\t            warn!(\n226\t                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n227\t                \"search UI rate limited (local backend)\"\n228\t            );\n229\t            return Err(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS);\n230\t        }\n231\t    }\n232\t\n233\t    // Resolve alias to concrete index UID (plan §13.7)\n234\t    // Aliases are resolved at routing time; resolved index is used for scatter planning\n235\t    let (effective_index, resolved_targets) = if state.config.aliases.enabled {\n236\t        let targets = state.alias_registry.resolve(&index).await;\n237\t        state.metrics.inc_alias_resolution(&index);\n238\t        if targets != vec![index.clone()] {\n239\t            // It's an alias\n240\t            (index.clone(), targets)\n241\t        } else {\n242\t            // Not an alias - use index directly\n243\t            (index.clone(), vec![index.clone()])\n244\t        }\n245\t    } else {\n246\t        // Aliases disabled - use index directly\n247\t        (index.clone(), vec![index.clone()])\n248\t    };\n249\t\n250\t    // Session pinning logic (plan §13.6): Check if session has pending write\n251\t    let (pinned_group, strategy_label) = if let Some(ref sid) = sid {\n252\t        if let Some(group) = state.session_manager.get_pinned_group(sid).await {\n253\t            // Session has a pending write - apply wait strategy\n254\t            let strategy = state.session_manager.wait_strategy();\n255\t            match strategy {\n256\t                WaitStrategy::Block => {\n257\t                    // Block until write completes or timeout\n258\t                    let max_wait = state.session_manager.max_wait_duration();\n259\t                    let wait_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n260\t                    match state.session_manager.wait_for_write_completion(\n261\t                        sid,\n262\t                        &state.task_registry,\n263\t                        max_wait,\n264\t                    ).await {\n265\t                        Ok(true) => {\n266\t                            // Write succeeded, clear pin and use normal routing\n267\t                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n268\t                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n269\t                            state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"block\");\n270\t                            (None, Some(\"block_success\"))\n271\t                        }\n272\t                        Ok(false) => {\n273\t                            // Write failed, clear pin\n274\t                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n275\t                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n276\t                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n277\t                            (None, Some(\"block_failed\"))\n278\t                        }\n279\t                        Err(_) => {\n280\t                            // Timeout - still route to pinned group (best effort)\n281\t                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n282\t                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n283\t                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n284\t                            (Some(group), Some(\"block_timeout\"))\n285\t                        }\n286\t                    }\n287\t                }\n288\t                WaitStrategy::RoutePin => {\n289\t                    // Route to pinned group without waiting\n290\t                    state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"route_pin\");\n291\t                    (Some(group), Some(\"route_pin\"))\n292\t                }\n293\t            }\n294\t        } else {\n295\t            // No pending write - normal routing\n296\t            (None, None)\n297\t        }\n298\t    } else {\n299\t        // No session header - normal routing\n300\t        (None, None)\n301\t    };\n302\t\n303\t    // Log session pinning decision for observability\n304\t    if let Some(label) = strategy_label {\n305\t        debug!(\n306\t            session_id = ?sid,\n307\t            pinned_group = pinned_group,\n308\t            strategy = label,\n309\t            \"session pinning applied\"\n310\t        );\n311\t    }\n312\t\n313\t    // Query coalescing (plan §13.10): Check for identical in-flight queries\n314\t    // Skip for multi-target aliases (each target is different)\n315\t    if state.config.query_coalescing.enabled && resolved_targets.len() == 1 {\n316\t        // Build fingerprint from canonicalized query body + index + settings version\n317\t        let settings_version = state.settings_broadcast.current_version().await;\n318\t        let query_json = serde_json::to_string(&body).unwrap_or_default();\n319\t        let fingerprint = QueryFingerprint {\n320\t            index: effective_index.clone(),\n321\t            query_json,\n322\t            settings_version,\n323\t        };\n324\t\n325\t        // Try to coalesce with an existing in-flight query\n326\t        if let Some(mut rx) = state.query_coalescer.try_coalesce(fingerprint.clone()).await {\n327\t            // Successfully subscribed to an in-flight query - wait for its result\n328\t            state.metrics.inc_query_coalesce_hits();\n329\t            debug!(\n330\t                index = %effective_index,\n331\t                \"query coalesced: waiting for in-flight query result\"\n332\t            );\n333\t\n334\t            // Wait for the response (with timeout matching the scatter timeout)\n335\t            let timeout = Duration::from_millis(state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms);\n336\t            let response_bytes = tokio::time::timeout(timeout, async move {\n337\t                rx.recv().await\n338\t            }).await;\n339\t\n340\t            match response_bytes {\n341\t                Ok(Ok(response_bytes)) => {\n342\t                    // Received response from coalesced query\n343\t                    let response_body: Value = match serde_json::from_slice(&response_bytes) {\n344\t                        Ok(v) => v,\n345\t                        Err(e) => {\n346\t                            error!(error = %e, \"failed to deserialize coalesced query response\");\n347\t                            return Err(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR);\n348\t                        }\n349\t                    };\n350\t\n351\t                    // Build response with appropriate headers\n352\t                    let mut response = Response::builder()\n353\t                        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n354\t                        .header(\"content-type\", \"application/json\");\n355\t\n356\t                    // Add settings headers\n357\t                    if state.settings_broadcast.is_in_flight(&effective_index).await {\n358\t                        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent\", \"true\");\n359\t                    }\n360\t                    if settings_version > 0 {\n361\t                        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Version\", settings_version.to_string());\n362\t                    }\n363\t\n364\t                    let response = response\n365\t                        .body(axum::body::Body::from(serde_json::to_string(&response_body).unwrap()))\n366\t                        .unwrap();\n367\t\n368\t                    tracing::info!(\n369\t                        target: \"miroir.search_coalesced\",\n370\t                        index = %effective_index,\n371\t                        duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n372\t                        \"coalesced search completed\"\n373\t                    );\n374\t\n375\t                    return Ok(response);\n376\t                }\n377\t                Ok(Err(_)) => {\n378\t                    // Channel closed without receiving response - proceed with normal scatter\n379\t                    debug!(\"coalesced query channel closed, proceeding with normal scatter\");\n380\t                }\n381\t                Err(_) => {\n382\t                    // Timeout waiting for coalesced query - proceed with normal scatter\n383\t                    debug!(\"timeout waiting for coalesced query, proceeding with normal scatter\");\n384\t                }\n385\t            }\n386\t        }\n387\t    }\n388\t\n389\t    // Handle multi-target alias fanout (plan §13.7, §13.11, §13.17)\n390\t    // Multi-target aliases (ILM read_alias) require fanning out to all targets\n391\t    // and merging results by _rankingScore\n392\t    if resolved_targets.len() > 1 {\n393\t        return search_multi_targets(\n394\t            resolved_targets,\n395\t            body,\n396\t            state,\n397\t            headers,\n398\t            sid,\n399\t            client_requested_score,\n400\t        ).await;\n401\t    }\n402\t\n403\t    // Get the scoped key for this index (plan §13.21).\n404\t    // If a scoped key exists, use primary_key (or previous_key during rotation overlap).\n405\t    // If no scoped key exists yet, fall back to node_master_key for initial setup.\n406\t    let search_key = if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n407\t        if let Ok(Some(sk)) = redis.get_search_ui_scoped_key(&effective_index) {\n408\t            // Refresh scoped-key beacon so the rotation leader knows this pod is serving\n409\t            // requests for this index at the current generation (plan §13.21).\n410\t            let _ = redis.observe_search_ui_scoped_key(\n411\t                &state.pod_id,\n412\t                &index,\n413\t                sk.generation,\n414\t            );\n415\t\n416\t            // Use primary_key; previous_key is the overlap fallback (both are valid in Meilisearch)\n417\t            sk.primary_key\n418\t        } else {\n419\t            // No scoped key yet — fall back to node_master_key for initial setup\n420\t            state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n421\t        }\n422\t    } else {\n423\t        // No Redis store — fall back to node_master_key (single-pod dev mode)\n424\t        state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n425\t    };\n426\t\n427\t    // Extract X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version header (plan §13.5)\n428\t    let min_settings_version = headers\n429\t        .get(\"X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version\")\n430\t        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n431\t        .and_then(|s| s.parse::<u64>().ok());\n432\t\n433\t    // Use live topology from shared state (updated by health checker)\n434\t    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n435\t    let policy = match state.config.scatter.unavailable_shard_policy.as_str() {\n436\t        \"partial\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n437\t        \"error\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Error,\n438\t        \"fallback\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback,\n439\t        _ => return Err(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR),\n440\t    };\n441\t\n442\t    // Plan scatter using live topology (span for plan construction)\n443\t    let plan = {\n444\t        let _plan_span = tracing::info_span!(\n445\t            \"scatter_plan\",\n446\t            replica_groups = state.config.replica_groups,\n447\t            shards = state.config.shards,\n448\t            rf = state.config.replication_factor,\n449\t            min_settings_version,\n450\t            pinned_group = ?pinned_group,\n451\t            strategy = %state.config.replica_selection.strategy,\n452\t        ).entered();\n453\t\n454\t        // Determine if we should use adaptive selection\n455\t        let use_adaptive = state.config.replica_selection.strategy == \"adaptive\";\n456\t        let replica_selector_ref = if use_adaptive {\n457\t            Some(state.replica_selector.as_ref())\n458\t        } else {\n459\t            None\n460\t        };\n461\t\n462\t        // Session pinning: if pinned_group is set, use group-specific planning\n463\t        if let Some(group) = pinned_group {\n464\t            match plan_search_scatter_for_group(\n465\t                &topo,\n466\t                0,\n467\t                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n468\t                state.config.shards,\n469\t                group,\n470\t                replica_selector_ref,\n471\t            ).await {\n472\t                Some(p) => p,\n473\t                None => {\n474\t                    // Pinned group not available - fall back to normal planning\n475\t                    // This can happen if the pinned group has failed\n476\t                    warn!(\n477\t                        pinned_group = group,\n478\t                        \"pinned group unavailable, falling back to normal routing\"\n479\t                    );\n480\t                    plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards, replica_selector_ref).await\n481\t                }\n482\t            }\n483\t        } else if let Some(floor) = min_settings_version {\n484\t            // If client provided a min settings version floor, use version-filtered planning\n485\t            // Clone the settings broadcast for version checking\n486\t            let settings_broadcast = state.settings_broadcast.clone();\n487\t            let plan_result = plan_search_scatter_with_version_floor(\n488\t                &topo,\n489\t                0,\n490\t                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n491\t                state.config.shards,\n492\t                &index,\n493\t                floor,\n494\t                &move |idx, node_id| {\n495\t                    // Use a blocking task wrapper since we're in a sync context\n496\t                    let sb = settings_broadcast.clone();\n497\t                    let idx = idx.to_string();\n498\t                    let node_id = node_id.to_string();\n499\t                    tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n500\t                        tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(async {\n501\t                            sb.node_version(&idx, &node_id).await\n502\t                        })\n503\t                    })\n504\t                },\n505\t                replica_selector_ref,\n506\t            ).await;\n507\t\n508\t            match plan_result {\n509\t                Some(p) => p,\n510\t                None => {\n511\t                    // No covering set could be assembled after filtering by version floor\n512\t                    let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n513\t                        MiroirCode::SettingsVersionStale,\n514\t                        format!(\n515\t                            \"no covering set available for settings version floor {} on index '{}'\",\n516\t                            floor, index\n517\t                        ),\n518\t                    );\n519\t                    return Err(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE);\n520\t                }\n521\t            }\n522\t        } else {\n523\t            // No version floor requested, use normal planning\n524\t            plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards, replica_selector_ref).await\n525\t        }\n526\t    };\n527\t    let node_count = plan.shard_to_node.len() as u64;\n528\t\n529\t    // Record scatter fan-out size before executing\n530\t    state.metrics.record_scatter_fan_out(node_count);\n531\t\n532\t    // Build search request\n533\t    let search_req = SearchRequest {\n534\t        index_uid: effective_index.clone(),\n535\t        query: body.q,\n536\t        offset: body.offset.unwrap_or(0),\n537\t        limit: body.limit.unwrap_or(20),\n538\t        filter: body.filter,\n539\t        facets: body.facets,\n540\t        ranking_score: client_requested_score,\n541\t        body: body.rest,\n542\t        global_idf: None,\n543\t    };\n544\t\n545\t    // Create node client with the scoped key (or node_master_key as fallback)\n546\t    let http_client = Arc::new(crate::client::HttpClient::new(\n547\t        search_key,\n548\t        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n549\t    ));\n550\t    let client = ProxyNodeClient::new(http_client, state.metrics.clone(), None);\n551\t\n552\t    // Use score-based merge strategy (OP#4: requires global IDF)\n553\t    let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n554\t\n555\t    // Register for query coalescing (plan §13.10) - after try_coalesce, before scatter\n556\t    // Only register if coalescing is enabled and this is a single-target query\n557\t    let (tx, fingerprint) = if state.config.query_coalescing.enabled && resolved_targets.len() == 1 {\n558\t        let settings_version = state.settings_broadcast.current_version().await;\n559\t        let query_json = serde_json::to_string(&body).unwrap_or_default();\n560\t        let fp = QueryFingerprint {\n561\t            index: effective_index.clone(),\n562\t            query_json,\n563\t            settings_version,\n564\t        };\n565\t\n566\t        match state.query_coalescer.register(fp.clone()).await {\n567\t            Ok(broadcast_tx) => {\n568\t                state.metrics.inc_query_coalesce_subscribers(1); // First subscriber = the query itself\n569\t                (Some(broadcast_tx), Some(fp))\n570\t            }\n571\t            Err(_) => {\n572\t                // Failed to register (too many pending queries) - proceed without coalescing\n573\t                debug!(\"too many pending queries, proceeding without coalescing registration\");\n574\t                (None, None)\n575\t            }\n576\t        }\n577\t    } else {\n578\t        (None, None)\n579\t    };\n580\t\n581\t    // Execute DFS query-then-fetch\n582\t    let mut result = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n583\t        plan,\n584\t        &client,\n585\t        search_req,\n586\t        &topo,\n587\t        policy,\n588\t        &strategy,\n589\t    )\n590\t    .await\n591\t    .map_err(|e| {\n592\t        tracing::error!(error = %e, \"search failed\");\n593\t        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n594\t    })?;\n595\t\n596\t    // Drop topology lock before building response\n597\t    drop(topo);\n598\t\n599\t    // Strip internal fields from hits\n600\t    for hit in &mut result.hits {\n601\t        strip_internal_fields(hit, client_requested_score);\n602\t    }\n603\t\n604\t    // Build response body\n605\t    let response_body = serde_json::json!({\n606\t        \"hits\": result.hits,\n607\t        \"estimatedTotalHits\": result.estimated_total_hits,\n608\t        \"processingTimeMs\": result.processing_time_ms,\n609\t    });\n610\t\n611\t    // Only include facetDistribution if facets were requested\n612\t    let mut body = response_body.clone();\n613\t    if let Some(facets) = &result.facet_distribution {\n614\t        body[\"facetDistribution\"] = serde_json::to_value(facets).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n615\t    }\n616\t\n617\t    // Broadcast result to coalesced queries (plan §13.10)\n618\t    if let (Some(broadcast_tx), Some(fp)) = (tx, fingerprint) {\n619\t        let response_bytes = serde_json::to_vec(&body).unwrap_or_default();\n620\t        let subscriber_count = broadcast_tx.receiver_count();\n621\t        if subscriber_count > 1 {\n622\t            // There are other queries waiting for this result - broadcast to them\n623\t            state.metrics.inc_query_coalesce_subscribers(subscriber_count as u64 - 1);\n624\t            let _ = broadcast_tx.send(response_bytes.clone());\n625\t            debug!(\n626\t                index = %effective_index,\n627\t                subscribers = subscriber_count - 1,\n628\t                \"broadcast search result to coalesced queries\"\n629\t            );\n630\t        }\n631\t        // Unregister the query after broadcasting\n632\t        state.query_coalescer.unregister(&fp).await;\n633\t    }\n634\t\n635\t    // Build response with optional headers\n636\t    let mut response = Response::builder()\n637\t        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n638\t        .header(\"content-type\", \"application/json\");\n639\t\n640\t    // Add X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent header if a broadcast is in flight (plan §13.5)\n641\t    if state.settings_broadcast.is_in_flight(&effective_index).await {\n642\t        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent\", \"true\");\n643\t    }\n644\t\n645\t    // Add X-Miroir-Settings-Version header if we have a version for this index\n646\t    let current_version = state.settings_broadcast.current_version().await;\n647\t    if current_version > 0 {\n648\t        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Version\", current_version.to_string());\n649\t    }\n650\t\n651\t    if result.degraded {\n652\t        state.metrics.inc_scatter_partial_responses();\n653\t    }\n654\t\n655\t    if result.degraded && !result.failed_shards.is_empty() {\n656\t        let mut sorted_shards = result.failed_shards.clone();\n657\t        sorted_shards.sort();\n658\t        let shard_ids = sorted_shards.iter()\n659\t            .map(|id| id.to_string())\n660\t            .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n661\t            .join(\",\");\n662\t        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", format!(\"shards={}\", shard_ids));\n663\t    } else if result.degraded {\n664\t        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", \"partial\");\n665\t    }\n666\t\n667\t    let response = response\n668\t        .body(axum::body::Body::from(serde_json::to_string(&body).unwrap()))\n669\t        .unwrap();\n670\t\n671\t    // Structured log entry (plan §10 shape)\n672\t    // request_id and pod_id are included from the middleware span via\n673\t    // .with_current_span(true) on the JSON subscriber layer.\n674\t    tracing::info!(\n675\t        target: \"miroir.search\",\n676\t        index = %effective_index,\n677\t        duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n678\t        node_count = node_count,\n679\t        estimated_hits = result.estimated_total_hits,\n680\t        degraded = result.degraded,\n681\t        \"search completed\"\n682\t    );\n683\t\n684\t    Ok(response)\n685\t}\n686\t\n687\t/// Search multiple target indexes (for multi-target aliases, plan §13.7, §13.11, §13.17).\n688\t///\n689\t/// Fans out the search to all target indexes and merges results by _rankingScore.\n690\t/// Used by ILM read_alias queries.\n691\tasync fn search_multi_targets(\n692\t    targets: Vec<String>,\n693\t    body: SearchRequestBody,\n694\t    state: Arc<AppState>,\n695\t    headers: HeaderMap,\n696\t    session_id: Option<String>,\n697\t    client_requested_score: bool,\n698\t) -> Result<Response, StatusCode> {\n699\t    let start = Instant::now();\n700\t\n701\t    // Extract session ID if provided\n702\t    let sid = session_id;\n703\t\n704\t    // Extract source IP from X-Forwarded-For or X-Real-IP (trust proxy)\n705\t    let source_ip = headers\n706\t        .get(\"x-forwarded-for\")\n707\t        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n708\t        .and_then(|s| s.split(',').next())\n709\t        .or_else(|| headers.get(\"x-real-ip\").and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok()))\n710\t        .unwrap_or(\"unknown\")\n711\t
