parse_rate_limit(&state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip) {\n716\t        Ok(parsed) => parsed,\n717\t        Err(e) => {\n718\t            warn!(error = %e, \"invalid search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip config, using default\");\n719\t            (60, 60) // Default: 60 requests per minute\n720\t        }\n721\t    };\n722\t\n723\t    let backend = state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.backend.as_str();\n724\t    if backend == \"redis\" {\n725\t        if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n726\t            match redis.check_rate_limit_search_ui(&source_ip, limit, window_seconds) {\n727\t                Ok((allowed, _wait_seconds)) => {\n728\t                    if !allowed {\n729\t                        warn!(\n730\t                            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n731\t                            \"search UI rate limited (redis)\"\n732\t                        );\n733\t                        return Err(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS);\n734\t                    }\n735\t                    // Allowed, proceed\n736\t                }\n737\t                Err(e) => {\n738\t                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to check search UI rate limit\");\n739\t                    // Continue anyway on error (fail-open)\n740\t                }\n741\t            }\n742\t        }\n743\t    } else if backend == \"local\" {\n744\t        let (allowed, _wait_seconds) = state.local_search_ui_rate_limiter.check(\n745\t            &source_ip,\n746\t            limit,\n747\t            window_seconds * 1000,\n748\t        );\n749\t        if !allowed {\n750\t            warn!(\n751\t                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n752\t                \"search UI rate session has pending write\n759\t    let (pinned_group, _strategy_label) = if let Some(ref sid) = sid {\n760\t        if let Some(group) = state.session_manager.get_pinned_group(sid).await {\n761\t            // Session has a pending write - apply wait strategy\n762\t            let strategy = state.session_manager.wait_strategy();\n763\t            match strategy {\n764\t                WaitStrategy::Block => {\n765\t                    // Block until write completes or timeout\n766\t                    let max_wait = state.session_manager.max_wait_duration();\n767\t &state.task_registry,\n771\t                        max_wait,\n772\t                    ).await {\n773\t                        Ok(true) => {\n774\t                            // Write succeeded, state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n777\t                            state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"block\");\n778\t                            (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n779\t                        }\n780\t                        Ok(false) => {\n781\t                            // Write failed, clear pin\n782\t                            let state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n785\t                            (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n786\t                        }\n787\t                        Err(_) => {\n788\t                            // Timeout - route to pinned group (best effort)\n789\t                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n790\t                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n791\t                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n792\t                            (Some(group), compatible settings (managed by ILM)\n813\t    let primary_target = targets.first().ok_or_else(|| {\n814\t        error!(\"multi-target alias has no targets\");\n815\t        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n816\t    })?;\n817\t\n818\t    // Get the scoped key for the primary target\n819\t    let search_key = if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n820\t        if &state.pod_id,\n824\t                primary_target,\n825\t                sk.generation,\n826\t            );\n827\t            sk.primary_key\n828\t        } else {\n829\t \"fallback\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback,\n847\t        _ => return Err(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR),\n848\t    };\n849\t\n850\t    // Plan scatter for primary target (for ILM read ).entered();\n863\t\n864\t        if let Some(group) = pinned_group {\n865\t            match plan_search_scatter_for_group(\n866\t                &topo,\n867\t                0,\n868\t self.metrics.set_replica_selection_score(node_id, score);\n    }\n\n    fn report_exploration(&self) {\n        self.metrics.inc_replica_selection_exploration();\n    }\n}\n\n/// Hash a value for logging (obfuscates sensitive data like IPs).\nfn hash_for_log(value: &str) -> String {\n    use std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\n    let mut hasher = std::collections::hash_map::DefaultHasher::new();\n    value.hash(&mut hasher);\n    format!(\"{:016x}\", hasher.finish())\n}\n\n/// Node client implementation using the HTTP client.\n///\n/// Wraps each node call with Prometheus metrics recording for\n/// `miroir_node_request_duration_seconds` and `miroir_node_errors_total`.\npub struct ProxyNodeClient {\n    client: Arc<crate::client::HttpClient>,\n    metrics: crate::middleware::Metrics,\n    replica_selector: Option<Arc<miroir_core::replica_selection::ReplicaSelector>>,\n}\n\nimpl ProxyNodeClient {\n    pub fn new(\n        client: Arc<crate::client::HttpClient>,\n        metrics: }\n}\n\n#[allow(async_fn_in_trait)]\nimpl NodeClient for ProxyNodeClient {\n    async fn search_node(\n        &self,\n        node: &miroir_core::topology::NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &SearchRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<Value, miroir_core::scatter::NodeError> {\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        let result = self.client.search_node(node, address, request).await;\n        let elapsed = start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n        self.metrics.record_node_request_duration(node.as_str(), \"search\", elapsed);\n        if let Err(ref e) = result {\n            self.metrics.inc_node_errors(node.as_str(), error_label(e));\n        }\n        result\n    }\n\n    async fn preflight_node(\n        &self,\n        node: &miroir_core::topology::NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &miroir_core::scatter::PreflightRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<miroir_core::scatter::PreflightResponse, miroir_core::scatter::NodeError> {\n        let self.metrics.record_node_request_duration(node.as_str(), \"preflight\", elapsed);\n        if let Err(ref e) = result {\n            self.metrics.inc_node_errors(node.as_str(), error_label(e));\n        }\n        result\n    }\n}\n\nfn error_label(e: &miroir_core::scatter::NodeError) -> &'static str {\n    match e {\n        miroir_core::scatter::NodeError::NetworkError(_) => \"network\",\n        miroir_core::scatter::NodeError::HttpError { .. } => \"http\",\n        miroir_core::scatter::NodeError::Timeout => \"timeout\",\n    }\n}\n\npub fn router<S>() -> axum::Router<S>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n{\n    axum::Router::new()\n        .route(\"/:index\", axum::routing::post(search_handler))\n}\n\n/// Search request body.\n#[derive(Deserialize, Serialize)]\nstruct SearchRequestBody {\n    q: Option<String>,\n    offset: Option<usize>,\n    limit: Option<usize>,\n    filter: Option<Value>,\n    facets: Option<Vec<String>>,\n    #[serde(rename = \"rankingScore\")]\n    ranking_score: Option<bool>,\n    #[serde(flatten)]\n    rest: Value,\n}\n\nimpl std::fmt::Debug for SearchRequestBody {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n        f.debug_struct(\"SearchRequestBody\")\n            .field(\"q\", &\"[redacted]\")\n            .field(\"offset\", &self.offset)\n            .field(\"limit\", &self.limit)\n            .field(\"filter\", &\"[redacted]\")\n            .field(\"facets\", &self.facets)\n            .field(\"ranking_score\", &self.ranking_score)\n            .finish_non_exhaustive()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Search handler with DFS global-IDF preflight (OP#4).\n///\n/// This handler implements the `dfs_query_then_fetch` pattern:\n/// 1. **Preflight phase**: Send term-frequency query to all shards, aggregate\n///    global document frequencies at the coordinator.\n/// 2. **Search phase**: Send the search query with global IDF attached so that\n///    scoring uses corpus-wide statistics instead of per-shard local IDF.\n///\n/// Returns `X-Miroir-Degraded: shards=X,Y,Z` header when any shards are unavailable.\n/// Strips `_miroir_shard` from all hits; strips `_rankingScore` unless client\n/// explicitly requested it.\n///\n/// Session pinning (plan §13.6): If `X-Miroir-Session` header is present and\n/// the session has a pending write, routes to the pinned group for read-your-writes.\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index))]\nasync fn search_handler(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    headers: HeaderMap,\n    session_id: Option<Extension<SessionId>>,\n    Json(body): Json<SearchRequestBody>,\n) -> Result<Response, StatusCode> {\n    let start = Instant::now();\n    let client_requested_score = body.ranking_score.unwrap_or(false);\n\n    // Extract session ID from request extensions (set by session_pinning_middleware)\n    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n        let s = ext.0;\n        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n    });\n\n    // Extract source IP from X-Forwarded-For or X-Real-IP (trust proxy)\n    let source_ip = headers\n        .get(\"x-forwarded-for\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .and_then(|s| s.split(',').next())\n        .or_else(|| headers.get(\"x-real-ip\").and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok()))\n        .unwrap_or(\"unknown\")\n        .trim()\n        .to_string();\n\n    // Check rate limit for search UI (plan §4)\n    let (limit, window_seconds) = match parse_rate_limit(&state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip) {\n        Ok(parsed) => parsed,\n        Err(e) => {\n            warn!(error = %e, \"invalid search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip config, using default\");\n            (60, 60) // Default: 60 requests per minute\n        }\n    };\n\n    let backend = state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.backend.as_str();\n    if backend == \"redis\" {\n        if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n            match redis.check_rate_limit_search_ui(&source_ip, limit, window_seconds) {\n                Ok((allowed, _wait_seconds)) => {\n                    if !allowed {\n                        warn!(\n                            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n                            \"search UI rate limited (redis)\"\n                        );\n                        return Err(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS);\n                    }\n                    // Allowed, proceed\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to check search UI rate limit\");\n                    // Continue anyway on error (fail-open)\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    } else if backend == \"local\" {\n        let (allowed, _wait_seconds) = state.local_search_ui_rate_limiter.check(\n            &source_ip,\n            limit,\n            window_seconds * 1000,\n        );\n        if !allowed {\n            warn!(\n                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n                \"search UI rate limited (local backend)\"\n            );\n            return Err(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Resolve alias to concrete index UID (plan §13.7)\n    // Aliases are resolved at routing time; resolved index is used for scatter planning\n    let (effective_index, resolved_targets) = if state.config.aliases.enabled {\n        let targets = state.alias_registry.resolve(&index).await;\n        state.metrics.inc_alias_resolution(&index);\n        if targets != vec![index.clone()] {\n            // It's an alias\n            (index.clone(), targets)\n        } else {\n            // Not an alias - use index directly\n            (index.clone(), vec![index.clone()])\n        }\n    } else {\n        // Aliases disabled - use index directly\n        (index.clone(), vec![index.clone()])\n    };\n\n    // Session pinning logic (plan §13.6): Check if session has pending write\n    let (pinned_group, strategy_label) = if let Some(ref sid) = sid {\n        if let Some(group) = state.session_manager.get_pinned_group(sid).await {\n            // Session has a pending write - apply wait strategy\n            let strategy = state.session_manager.wait_strategy();\n            match strategy {\n                WaitStrategy::Block => {\n                    // Block until write completes or timeout\n                    let max_wait = state.session_manager.max_wait_duration();\n                    let wait_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n                    match state.session_manager.wait_for_write_completion(\n                        sid,\n                        &state.task_registry,\n                        max_wait,\n                    ).await {\n                        Ok(true) => {\n                            // Write succeeded, clear pin and use normal routing\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"block\");\n                            (None, Some(\"block_success\"))\n                        }\n                        Ok(false) => {\n                            // Write failed, clear pin\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n                            (None, Some(\"block_failed\"))\n                        }\n                        Err(_) => {\n                            // Timeout - still route to pinned group (best effort)\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n                            (Some(group), Some(\"block_timeout\"))\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                WaitStrategy::RoutePin => {\n                    // Route to pinned group without waiting\n                    state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"route_pin\");\n                    (Some(group), Some(\"route_pin\"))\n                }\n            }\n        } else {\n            // No pending write - normal routing\n            (None, None)\n        }\n    } else {\n        // No session header - normal routing\n        (None, None)\n    };\n\n    // Log session pinning decision for observability\n    if let Some(label) = strategy_label {\n        debug!(\n            session_id = ?sid,\n            pinned_group = pinned_group,\n            strategy = label,\n            \"session pinning applied\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Query coalescing (plan §13.10): Check for identical in-flight queries\n    // Skip for multi-target aliases (each target is different)\n    if state.config.query_coalescing.enabled && resolved_targets.len() == 1 {\n        // Build fingerprint from canonicalized query body + index + settings version\n        let settings_version = state.settings_broadcast.current_version().await;\n        let query_json = serde_json::to_string(&body).unwrap_or_default();\n        let fingerprint = QueryFingerprint {\n            index: effective_index.clone(),\n            query_json,\n            settings_version,\n        };\n\n        // Try to coalesce with an existing in-flight query\n        if let Some(mut rx) = state.query_coalescer.try_coalesce(fingerprint.clone()).await {\n            // Successfully subscribed to an in-flight query - wait for its result\n            state.metrics.inc_query_coalesce_hits();\n            debug!(\n                index = %effective_index,\n                \"query coalesced: waiting for in-flight query result\"\n            );\n\n            // Wait for the response (with timeout matching the scatter timeout)\n            let timeout = Duration::from_millis(state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms);\n            let response_bytes = tokio::time::timeout(timeout, async move {\n                rx.recv().await\n            }).await;\n\n            match response_bytes {\n                Ok(Ok(response_bytes)) => {\n                    // Received response from coalesced query\n                    let response_body: Value = match serde_json::from_slice(&response_bytes) {\n                        Ok(v) => v,\n                        Err(e) => {\n                            error!(error = %e, \"failed to deserialize coalesced query response\");\n                            return Err(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR);\n                        }\n                    };\n\n                    // Build response with appropriate headers\n                    let mut response = Response::builder()\n                        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n                        .header(\"content-type\", \"application/json\");\n\n                    // Add settings headers\n                    if state.settings_broadcast.is_in_flight(&effective_index).await {\n                        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent\", \"true\");\n                    }\n                    if settings_version > 0 {\n                        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Version\", settings_version.to_string());\n                    }\n\n                    let response = response\n                        .body(axum::body::Body::from(serde_json::to_string(&response_body).unwrap()))\n                        .unwrap();\n\n                    tracing::info!(\n                        target: \"miroir.search_coalesced\",\n                        index = %effective_index,\n                        duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n                        \"coalesced search completed\"\n                    );\n\n                    return Ok(response);\n                }\n                Ok(Err(_)) => {\n                    // Channel closed without receiving response - proceed with normal scatter\n                    debug!(\"coalesced query channel closed, proceeding with normal scatter\");\n                }\n                Err(_) => {\n                    // Timeout waiting for coalesced query - proceed with normal scatter\n                    debug!(\"timeout waiting for coalesced query, proceeding with normal scatter\");\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Handle multi-target alias fanout (plan §13.7, §13.11, §13.17)\n    // Multi-target aliases (ILM read_alias) require fanning out to all targets\n    // and merging results by _rankingScore\n    if resolved_targets.len() > 1 {\n        return search_multi_targets(\n            resolved_targets,\n            body,\n            state,\n            headers,\n            sid,\n            client_requested_score,\n        ).await;\n    }\n\n    // Get the scoped key for this index (plan §13.21).\n    // If a scoped key exists, use primary_key (or previous_key during rotation overlap).\n    // If no scoped key exists yet, fall back to node_master_key for initial setup.\n    let search_key = if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n        if let Ok(Some(sk)) = redis.get_search_ui_scoped_key(&effective_index) {\n            // Refresh scoped-key beacon so the rotation leader knows this pod is serving\n            // requests for this index at the current generation (plan §13.21).\n            let _ = redis.observe_search_ui_scoped_key(\n                &state.pod_id,\n                &index,\n                sk.generation,\n            );\n\n            // Use primary_key; previous_key is the overlap fallback (both are valid in Meilisearch)\n            sk.primary_key\n        } else {\n            // No scoped key yet — fall back to node_master_key for initial setup\n            state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n        }\n    } else {\n        // No Redis store — fall back to node_master_key (single-pod dev mode)\n        state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n    };\n\n    // Extract X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version header (plan §13.5)\n    let min_settings_version = headers\n        .get(\"X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .and_then(|s| s.parse::<u64>().ok());\n\n    // Use live topology from shared state (updated by health checker)\n    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n    let policy = match state.config.scatter.unavailable_shard_policy.as_str() {\n        \"partial\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n        \"error\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Error,\n        \"fallback\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback,\n        _ => return Err(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR),\n    };\n\n    // Plan scatter using live topology (span for plan construction)\n    let plan = {\n        let _plan_span = tracing::info_span!(\n            \"scatter_plan\",\n            replica_groups = state.config.replica_groups,\n            shards = state.config.shards,\n            rf = state.config.replication_factor,\n            min_settings_version,\n            pinned_group = ?pinned_group,\n            strategy = %state.config.replica_selection.strategy,\n        ).entered();\n\n        // Determine if we should use adaptive selection\n        let use_adaptive = state.config.replica_selection.strategy == \"adaptive\";\n        let replica_selector_ref = if use_adaptive {\n            Some(state.replica_selector.as_ref())\n        } else {\n            None\n        };\n\n        // Session pinning: if pinned_group is set, use group-specific planning\n        if let Some(group) = pinned_group {\n            match plan_search_scatter_for_group(\n                &topo,\n                0,\n                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n                state.config.shards,\n                group,\n                replica_selector_ref,\n            ).await {\n                Some(p) => p,\n                None => {\n                    // Pinned group not available - fall back to normal planning\n                    // This can happen if the pinned group has failed\n                    warn!(\n                        pinned_group = group,\n                        \"pinned group unavailable, falling back to normal routing\"\n                    );\n                    plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards, replica_selector_ref).await\n                }\n            }\n        } else if let Some(floor) = min_settings_version {\n            // If client provided a min settings version floor, use version-filtered planning\n            // Clone the settings broadcast for version checking\n            let settings_broadcast = state.settings_broadcast.clone();\n            let plan_result = plan_search_scatter_with_version_floor(\n                &topo,\n                0,\n                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n                state.config.shards,\n                &index,\n                floor,\n                &move |idx, node_id| {\n                    // Use a blocking task wrapper since we're in a sync context\n                    let sb = settings_broadcast.clone();\n                    let idx = idx.to_string();\n                    let node_id = node_id.to_string();\n                    tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n                        tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(async {\n                            sb.node_version(&idx, &node_id).await\n                        })\n                    })\n                },\n                replica_selector_ref,\n            ).await;\n\n            match plan_result {\n                Some(p) => p,\n                None => {\n                    // No covering set could be assembled after filtering by version floor\n                    let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n                        MiroirCode::SettingsVersionStale,\n                        format!(\n                            \"no covering set available for settings version floor {} on index '{}'\",\n                            floor, index\n                        ),\n                    );\n                    return Err(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE);\n                }\n            }\n        } else {\n            // No version floor requested, use normal planning\n            plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards, replica_selector_ref).await\n        }\n    };\n    let node_count = plan.shard_to_node.len() as u64;\n\n    // Record scatter fan-out size before executing\n    state.metrics.record_scatter_fan_out(node_count);\n\n    // Build search request\n    let search_req = SearchRequest {\n        index_uid: effective_index.clone(),\n        query: body.q,\n        offset: body.offset.unwrap_or(0),\n        limit: body.limit.unwrap_or(20),\n        filter: body.filter,\n        facets: body.facets,\n        ranking_score: client_requested_score,\n        body: body.rest,\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n\n    // Create node client with the scoped key (or node_master_key as fallback)\n    let http_client = Arc::new(crate::client::HttpClient::new(\n        search_key,\n        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n    ));\n    let client = ProxyNodeClient::new(http_client, state.metrics.clone(), None);\n\n    // Use score-based merge strategy (OP#4: requires global IDF)\n    let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n\n    // Register for query coalescing (plan §13.10) - after try_coalesce, before scatter\n    // Only register if coalescing is enabled and this is a single-target query\n    let (tx, fingerprint) = if state.config.query_coalescing.enabled && resolved_targets.len() == 1 {\n        let settings_version = state.settings_broadcast.current_version().await;\n        let query_json = serde_json::to_string(&body).unwrap_or_default();\n        let fp = QueryFingerprint {\n            index: effective_index.clone(),\n            query_json,\n            settings_version,\n        };\n\n        match state.query_coalescer.register(fp.clone()).await {\n            Ok(broadcast_tx) => {\n                state.metrics.inc_query_coalesce_subscribers(1); // First subscriber = the query itself\n                (Some(broadcast_tx), Some(fp))\n            }\n            Err(_) => {\n                // Failed to register (too many pending queries) - proceed without coalescing\n                debug!(\"too many pending queries, proceeding without coalescing registration\");\n                (None, None)\n            }\n        }\n    } else {\n        (None, None)\n    };\n\n    // Execute DFS query-then-fetch\n    let mut result = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n        plan,\n        &client,\n        search_req,\n        &topo,\n        policy,\n        &strategy,\n    )\n    .await\n    .map_err(|e| {\n        tracing::error!(error = %e, \"search failed\");\n        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n    })?;\n\n    // Drop topology lock before building response\n    drop(topo);\n\n    // Strip internal fields from hits\n    for hit in &mut result.hits {\n        strip_internal_fields(hit, client_requested_score);\n    }\n\n    // Build response body\n    let response_body = serde_json::json!({\n        \"hits\": result.hits,\n        \"estimatedTotalHits\": result.estimated_total_hits,\n        \"processingTimeMs\": result.processing_time_ms,\n    });\n\n    // Only include facetDistribution if facets were requested\n    let mut body = response_body.clone();\n    if let Some(facets) = &result.facet_distribution {\n        body[\"facetDistribution\"] = serde_json::to_value(facets).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    }\n\n    // Broadcast result to coalesced queries (plan §13.10)\n    if let (Some(broadcast_tx), Some(fp)) = (tx, fingerprint) {\n        let response_bytes = serde_json::to_vec(&body).unwrap_or_default();\n        let subscriber_count = broadcast_tx.receiver_count();\n        if subscriber_count > 1 {\n            // There are other queries waiting for this result - broadcast to them\n            state.metrics.inc_query_coalesce_subscribers(subscriber_count as u64 - 1);\n            let _ = broadcast_tx.send(response_bytes.clone());\n            debug!(\n                index = %effective_index,\n                subscribers = subscriber_count - 1,\n                \"broadcast search result to coalesced queries\"\n            );\n        }\n        // Unregister the query after broadcasting\n        state.query_coalescer.unregister(&fp).await;\n    }\n\n    // Build response with optional headers\n    let mut response = Response::builder()\n        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n        .header(\"content-type\", \"application/json\");\n\n    // Add X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent header if a broadcast is in flight (plan §13.5)\n    if state.settings_broadcast.is_in_flight(&effective_index).await {\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent\", \"true\");\n    }\n\n    // Add X-Miroir-Settings-Version header if we have a version for this index\n    let current_version = state.settings_broadcast.current_version().await;\n    if current_version > 0 {\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Version\", current_version.to_string());\n    }\n\n    if result.degraded {\n        state.metrics.inc_scatter_partial_responses();\n    }\n\n    if result.degraded && !result.failed_shards.is_empty() {\n        let mut sorted_shards = result.failed_shards.clone();\n        sorted_shards.sort();\n        let shard_ids = sorted_shards.iter()\n            .map(|id| id.to_string())\n            .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n            .join(\",\");\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", format!(\"shards={}\", shard_ids));\n    } else if result.degraded {\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", \"partial\");\n    }\n\n    let response = response\n        .body(axum::body::Body::from(serde_json::to_string(&body).unwrap()))\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Structured log entry (plan §10 shape)\n    // request_id and pod_id are included from the middleware span via\n    // .with_current_span(true) on the JSON subscriber layer.\n    tracing::info!(\n        target: \"miroir.search\",\n        index = %effective_index,\n        duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n        node_count = node_count,\n        estimated_hits = result.estimated_total_hits,\n        degraded = result.degraded,\n        \"search completed\"\n    );\n\n    Ok(response)\n}\n\n/// Search multiple target indexes (for multi-target aliases, plan §13.7, §13.11, §13.17).\n///\n/// Fans out the search to all target indexes and merges results by _rankingScore.\n/// Used by ILM read_alias queries.\nasync fn search_multi_targets(\n    targets: Vec<String>,\n    body: SearchRequestBody,\n    state: Arc<AppState>,\n    headers: HeaderMap,\n    session_id: Option<String>,\n    client_requested_score: bool,\n) -> Result<Response, StatusCode> {\n    let start = Instant::now();\n\n    // Extract session ID if provided\n    let sid = session_id;\n\n    // Extract source IP from X-Forwarded-For or X-Real-IP (trust proxy)\n    let source_ip = headers\n        .get(\"x-forwarded-for\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .and_then(|s| s.split(',').next())\n        .or_else(|| headers.get(\"x-real-ip\").and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok()))\n        .unwrap_or(\"unknown\")\n        .trim()\n        .to_string();\n\n    // Check rate limit for search UI (plan §4)\n    let (limit, window_seconds) = match parse_rate_limit(&state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip) {\n        Ok(parsed) => parsed,\n        Err(e) => {\n            warn!(error = %e, \"invalid search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip config, using default\");\n            (60, 60) // Default: 60 requests per minute\n        }\n    };\n\n    let backend = state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.backend.as_str();\n    if backend == \"redis\" {\n        if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n            match redis.check_rate_limit_search_ui(&source_ip, limit, window_seconds) {\n                Ok((allowed, _wait_seconds)) => {\n                    if !allowed {\n                        warn!(\n                            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n                            \"search UI rate limited (redis)\"\n                        );\n                        return Err(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS);\n                    }\n                    // Allowed, proceed\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to check search UI rate limit\");\n                    // Continue anyway on error (fail-open)\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    } else if backend == \"local\" {\n        let (allowed, _wait_seconds) = state.local_search_ui_rate_limiter.check(\n            &source_ip,\n            limit,\n            window_seconds * 1000,\n        );\n        if !allowed {\n            warn!(\n                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n                \"search UI rate limited (local backend)\"\n            );\n            return Err(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Session pinning logic (plan §13.6): Check if session has pending write\n    let (pinned_group, _strategy_label) = if let Some(ref sid) = sid {\n        if let Some(group) = state.session_manager.get_pinned_group(sid).await {\n            // Session has a pending write - apply wait strategy\n            let strategy = state.session_manager.wait_strategy();\n            match strategy {\n                WaitStrategy::Block => {\n                    // Block until write completes or timeout\n                    let max_wait = state.session_manager.max_wait_duration();\n                    let wait_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n                    match state.session_manager.wait_for_write_completion(\n                        sid,\n                        &state.task_registry,\n                        max_wait,\n                    ).await {\n                        Ok(true) => {\n                            // Write succeeded, clear pin and use normal routing\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"block\");\n                            (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n                        }\n                        Ok(false) => {\n                            // Write failed, clear pin\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n                            (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n                        }\n                        Err(_) => {\n                            // Timeout - route to pinned group (best effort)\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n                            (Some(group), None::<u64>)\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                WaitStrategy::RoutePin => {\n                    // Route to pinned group without waiting\n                    state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"route_pin\");\n                    (Some(group), None::<u64>)\n                }\n            }\n        } else {\n            // No pending write - normal routing\n            (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n        }\n    } else {\n        // No session header - normal routing\n        (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n    };\n\n    // For multi-target aliases, we use the first target for settings/scoped key\n    // All targets should have compatible settings (managed by ILM)\n    let primary_target = targets.first().ok_or_else(|| {\n        error!(\"multi-target alias has no targets\");\n        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n    })?;\n\n    // Get the scoped key for the primary target\n    let search_key = if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n        if let Ok(Some(sk)) = redis.get_search_ui_scoped_key(primary_target) {\n            // Refresh scoped-key beacon\n            let _ = redis.observe_search_ui_scoped_key(\n                &state.pod_id,\n                primary_target,\n                sk.generation,\n            );\n            sk.primary_key\n        } else {\n            state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n        }\n    } else {\n        state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n    };\n\n    // Extract X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version header\n    let min_settings_version = headers\n        .get(\"X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .and_then(|s| s.parse::<u64>().ok());\n\n    // Use live topology from shared state\n    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n    let policy = match state.config.scatter.unavailable_shard_policy.as_str() {\n        \"partial\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n        \"error\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Error,\n        \"fallback\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback,\n        _ => return Err(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR),\n    };\n\n    // Plan scatter for primary target (for ILM read aliases, all targets\n    // should have identical shard layout)\n    let plan = {\n        let _plan_span = tracing::info_span!(\n            \"scatter_plan_multi_target\",\n            primary_target = %primary_target,\n            replica_groups = state.config.replica_groups,\n            shards = state.config.shards,\n            rf = state.config.replication_factor,\n            min_settings_version,\n            pinned_group = ?pinned_group,\n            target_count = targets.len(),\n        ).entered();\n\n        if let Some(group) = pinned_group {\n            match plan_search_scatter_for_group(\n                &topo,\n                0,\n                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n                state.config.shards,\n                group,\n                None,\n            ) {\n                Some(p) => p,\n                None => {\n                    warn!(\n                        pinned_group = group,\n                        \"pinned group unavailable, falling back to normal routing\"\n                    );\n                    plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards, None).await\n                }\n            }\n        } else if let Some(floor) = min_settings_version {\n            let settings_broadcast = state.settings_broadcast.clone();\n            let plan_result = plan_search_scatter_with_version_floor(\n                &topo,\n                0,\n                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n                state.config.shards,\n                primary_target,\n                floor,\n                &move |idx, node_id| {\n                    let sb = settings_broadcast.clone();\n                    let idx = idx.to_string();\n                    let node_id = node_id.to_string();\n                    tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n                        tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(async {\n                            sb.node_version(&idx, &node_id).await\n                        })\n                    })\n                },\n            );\n\n            match plan_result {\n                Some(p) => p,\n                None => {\n                    let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n                        MiroirCode::SettingsVersionStale,\n                        format!(\n                            \"no covering set available for settings version floor {} on index '{}'\",\n                            floor, primary_target\n                        ),\n                    );\n                    return Err(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE);\n                }\n            }\n        } else {\n            plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards)\n        }\n    };\n    let node_count = plan.shard_to_node.len() as u64;\n\n    // Record scatter fan-out size\n    state.metrics.record_scatter_fan_out(node_count);\n\n    // Build search request for primary target\n    let search_req = SearchRequest {\n        index_uid: primary_target.to_string(),\n        query: body.q.clone(),\n        offset: body.offset.unwrap_or(0),\n        limit: body.limit.unwrap_or(20),\n        filter: body.filter.clone(),\n        facets: body.facets.clone(),\n        ranking_score: client_requested_score,\n        body: body.rest.clone(),\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n\n    // Create node client\n    let http_client = Arc::new(crate::client::HttpClient::new(\n        search_key,\n        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n    ));\n    let client = ProxyNodeClient::new(http_client, state.metrics.clone(), None);\n\n    // Use score-based merge strategy\n    let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n\n    // Execute search\n    let mut result = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n        plan,\n        &client,\n        search_req,\n        &topo,\n        policy,\n        &strategy,\n    )\n    .await\n    .map_err(|e| {\n        tracing::error!(error = %e, \"multi-target search failed\");\n        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n    })?;\n\n    // Drop topology lock before building response\n    drop(topo);\n\n    // Strip internal fields from hits\n    for hit in &mut result.hits {\n        strip_internal_fields(hit, client_requested_score);\n    }\n\n    // Build response body\n    let mut response_body = serde_json::json!({\n        \"hits\": result.hits,\n        \"estimatedTotalHits\": result.estimated_total_hits,\n        \"processingTimeMs\": result.processing_time_ms,\n    });\n\n    // Only include facetDistribution if facets were requested\n    if let Some(facets) = &result.facet_distribution {\n        response_body[\"facetDistribution\"] = serde_json::to_value(facets).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    }\n\n    // Build response with optional headers\n    let mut response = Response::builder()\n        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n        .header(\"content-type\", \"application/json\");\n\n    // Add settings headers\n    if state.settings_broadcast.is_in_flight(primary_target).await {\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent\", \"true\");\n    }\n\n    let current_version = state.settings_broadcast.current_version().await;\n    if current_version > 0 {\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Version\", current_version.to_string());\n    }\n\n    if result.degraded {\n        state.metrics.inc_scatter_partial_responses();\n    }\n\n    if result.degraded && !result.failed_shards.is_empty() {\n        let mut sorted_shards = result.failed_shards.clone();\n        sorted_shards.sort();\n        let shard_ids = sorted_shards.iter()\n            .map(|id| id.to_string())\n            .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n            .join(\",\");\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", format!(\"shards={}\", shard_ids));\n    } else if result.degraded {\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", \"partial\");\n    }\n\n    let response = response\n        .body(axum::body::Body::from(serde_json::to_string(&response_body).unwrap()))\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Structured log entry\n    tracing::info!(\n        target: \"miroir.search_multi_target\",\n        primary_target = %primary_target,\n        target_count = targets.len(),\n        duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n        node_count = node_count,\n        estimated_hits = result.estimated_total_hits,\n        degraded = result.degraded,\n        \"multi-target search completed\"\n    );\n\n    Ok(response)\n}\n\n/// Strip `_miroir_shard` from all hits (always).\n/// Strip `_rankingScore` unless the client explicitly requested it.\npub fn strip_internal_fields(hit: &mut Value, client_requested_score: bool) {\n    if let Some(obj) = hit.as_object_mut() {\n        obj.remove(\"_miroir_shard\");\n        if !client_requested_score {\n            obj.remove(\"_rankingScore\");\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n {\n90\t        // Check cache first\n91\t        {\n92\t            let cache = self.version_cache.read().await;\n93\t            let last_update = self.last_cache_update.read().await;\n94\t          .timeout(Duration::from_secs(2))\n104\t            .build()\n105\t            .map_err(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)?;\n106\t\n107\t        for address in &self.node_addresses ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n134\t\n135\t/// In-memory rate limiter for admin login (local backend only).\n136\t/// Thread-safe using Arc<Mutex<...>>.\n137\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n138\tpub struct LocalAdminRateLimiter {\n139\t    inner: Arc<std::sync::Mutex<LocalAdminRateLimiterInner>>,\n140\t}\n141\t\n142\t#[derive(Debug, Default)]\n143\tstruct LocalAdminRateLimiterInner {\n144\t    /// Map of IP -> (request_timestamps_ms, failed_count, backoff_until_ms)\n145\t    state: HashMap<String, LocalRateLimitState>,\n146\t}\n147\t\n148\t#[derive(Debug, Default, Clone)]\n149\tstruct LocalRateLimitState {\n150\t    /// Timestamps of recent requests (for sliding window)\n151\t    request_timestamps_ms: Vec<i64>,\n152\t    /// Consecutive failed login attempts\n153\t    failed_count: u32,\n154\t    /// Unix timestamp (ms) when backoff expires\n155\t    backoff_until_ms: Option<i64>,\n156\t}\n157\t\n158\timpl LocalAdminRateLimiter {\n159\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n160\t        Self {\n161\t            inner: Arc::new(std::sync::Mutex::new(LocalAdminRateLimiterInner::default())),\n162\t        }\n163\t    }\n164\t\n165\t    /// Check rate limit and exponential backoff.\n166\t    /// Returns (allowed, wait_seconds).\n167\t    pub fn check(\n168\t        &self,\n169\t        ip: &str,\n170\t        limit: u64,\n171\t        window_ms: u64,\n172\t        failed_threshold: u32,\n173\t        backoff_start_minutes: u64,\n174\t        backoff_max_hours: u64,\n175\t    ) -> (bool, Option<u64>) {\n176\t        let mut inner = self.inner.lock().unwrap();\n177\t        let now = now_ms();\n178\t        let state = inner.state.entry(ip.to_string()).or_default();\n179\t\n180\t        // Check if we're in backoff mode\n181\t        if let Some(backoff_until) = state.backoff_until_ms {\n182\t            if backoff_until > now {\n183\t                let wait_seconds = ((backoff_until - now) / 1000) as u64;\n184\t                return (false, Some(wait_seconds));\n185\t            }\n186\t            // Backoff expired, clear it\n187\t            state.backoff_until_ms = None;\n188\t        }\n189\t\n190\t        // Clean old timestamps outside the window\n191\t        state.request_timestamps_ms.retain(|&ts| now - ts < window_ms as i64);\n192\t\n193\t        // Check if limit exceeded\n194\t        if state.request_timestamps_ms.len() >= limit as usize {\n195\t            // Enter backoff mode after threshold consecutive failures\n196\t            let failed = state.failed_count + 1;\n197\t            state.failed_count = failed;\n198\t\n199\t            if failed >= failed_threshold {\n200\t                let backoff_minutes = backoff_start_minutes * (1u64 << ((failed - failed_threshold) as u64).min(7)); // Cap at 2^7 = 128x\n201\t                let backoff_seconds = (backoff_minutes * 60).min(backoff_max_hours * 3600);\n202\t                state.backoff_until_ms = Some(now + (backoff_seconds as i64 * 1000));\n203\t                return (false, Some(backoff_seconds));\n204\t            }\n205\t\n206\t            return (false, None);\n207\t        }\n208\t\n209\t        // Record this request\n210\t        state.request_timestamps_ms.push(now);\n211\t        (true, None)\n212\t    }\n213\t\n214\t    /// Reset rate limit and backoff state on successful login.\n215\t    pub fn reset(&self, ip: &str) {\n216\t        let mut inner = self.inner.lock().unwrap();\n217\t        inner.state.remove(ip);\n218\t    }\n219\t\n220\t    /// Record a failed login attempt (for backoff calculation).\n221\t    pub fn record_failure(&self, ip: &str, failed_threshold: u32, backoff_start_minutes: u64, backoff_max_hours: u64) -> Option<u64> {\n222\t        let mut inner = self.inner.lock().unwrap();\n223\t        let now = now_ms();\n224\t        let state = inner.state.entry(ip.to_string()).or_default();\n225\t\n226\t        state.failed_count += 1;\n227\t\n228\t        if state.failed_count >= failed_threshold {\n229\t            let backoff_minutes = backoff_start_minutes * (1u64 << ((state.failed_count - failed_threshold) as u64).min(7));\n230\t            let backoff_seconds = (backoff_minutes * 60).min(backoff_max_hours * 3600);\n231\t            state.backoff_until_ms = Some(now + (backoff_seconds as i64 * 1000));\n232\t            return Some(backoff_seconds);\n233\t        }\n234\t\n235\t        None\n236\t    }\n237\t}\n238\t\n239\timpl Default for LocalAdminRateLimiter {\n240\t    fn default() -> Self {\n241\t        Self::new()\n242\t    }\n243\t}\n244\t\n245\t/// In-memory rate limiter for search UI (local backend only).\n246\t/// Thread-safe using Arc<Mutex<...>>.\n247\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n248\tpub struct }\n682\t\n683\t    /// Check if a covering quorum is reachable.\n684\t    pub async fn check_covering_quorum(&self) -> bool {\n685\t        let topo = self.topology.read().await;\n686\t        let node_map = topo.node_map();\n687\t\n688\t        // For each replica group, check if we have enough healthy nodes\n689\t        for group in topo.groups() {\n690\t            let healthy = group.healthy_nodes(&node_map);\n691\t            let required = (topo.rf() + 1) / 2; // Simple majority for quorum\n692\t            if healthy.len() < required {\n693\t                return false;\n694\t            }\n695\t        }\n696\t\n697\t        true\n698\t    }\n699\t\n700\t    /// Sync rebalancer metrics to Prometheus (called from health checker).\n701\t    pub async fn sync_rebalancer_metrics_to_prometheus(&self) {\n702\t        if let Some(ref rebalancer) = self.rebalancer {\n703\t            let rebalancer_metrics = rebalancer.metrics.read().await;\n704\t            let in_progress = rebalancer_metrics.rebalance_start_time.is_some();\n705\t            self.metrics.set_rebalance_in_progress(in_progress);\n706\t\n707\t            // Calculate delta for documents migrated counter\n708\t            let current_total = rebalancer_metrics.documents_migrated_total;\n709\t            let previous = self.previous_docs_migrated.load(std::sync::atomic::Ordering::Relaxed);\n710\t            if current_total > previous {\n711\t                let delta = current_total - previous;\n712\t                self.metrics.inc_rebalance_documents_migrated(delta);\n713\t                self.previous_docs_migrated.store(current_total, std::sync::atomic::Ordering::Relaxed);\n714\t            }\n715\t\n716\t            let duration = rebalancer_metrics.current_duration_secs();\n717\t            if duration > 0.0 {\n718\t                self.metrics.observe_rebalance_duration(duration);\n719\t fn get_topology<S>(State(state): State<S>) -> Result<Json<TopologyResponse>, StatusCode>\n755\twhere\n756\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n757\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n758\t{\n759\t    let n.status).to_lowercase(),\n780\t            shard_count: 0, // TODO: compute from routing table\n781\t            last_seen_ms: 0, // TODO: track last health check time\n782\t            error: None,     // TODO: populate from last health check error\n783\t        })\n784\t        .collect();\n785\t\n786\t    // Check if fully covered\n787\t    let fully_covered = degraded_count == 0;\n788\t\n789\t    let response = TopologyResponse {\n790\t        shards: topo.shards,\n791\t        replication_factor: topo.rf() as u32,\n792\t        nodes,\n793\t        degraded_node_count: table.\n802\tpub async fn get_shards<S>(State(state): State<S>) -> Result<Json<ShardsResponse>, StatusCode>\n803\twhere\n804\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n805\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n806\t{\n807\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n808\t    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n809\t    let mut shards = HashMap::new();\n810\t\n811\t    // Build shard -> node mapping using rendezvous hash\n812\t    for shard_id in 0..topo.shards {\n813\t        let mut node_ids = Vec::new();\n814\t\n815\t        // Collect nodes from all replica groups for this shard\n816\t        for group in topo.groups() {\n817\t            let assigned = router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topo.rf());\n818\t            for node_id in assigned {\n819\t                node_ids.push(node_id.as_str().to_string());\n820\t            }\n821\t        }\n822\t\n823\t        shards.insert(shard_id.to_string(), node_ids);\n824\t    }\n825\t\n826\t    Ok(Json(ShardsResponse { shards }))\n827\t}\n828\t\n829\t/// GET /_miroir/ready — readiness probe (503 during startup, 200 once ready).\n830\tpub async fn get_ready<S>(State(state): State<S>) -> Result<&'static str, StatusCode>\n831\twhere\n832\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n833\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n834\t{\n835\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n836\t    let ready = *state.ready.read().await;\n837\t\n838\t    if ready {\n839\t        Ok(\"\")\n840\t    } else {\n841\t        // Not yet marked ready - check if covering quorum exists\n842\t        let has_quorum = metrics.\n854\tpub async fn get_metrics<S>(State(state): State<S>) -> Response\n855\twhere\n856\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n857\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n858\t{\n859\t    let state = /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/rotate-scoped-key — manual rotation trigger.\n870\t///\n871\t/// Admin-gated endpoint that initiates a scoped key rotation for the given index.\n872\t/// Set `force: true` in the request body to bypass the timing gate.\n873\tpub async fn rotate_scoped_key_handler<S>(\n874\t    State(state): State<S>,\n875\t    Path(index): Path<String>,\n876\t    Json(body): Json<RotateScopedKeyRequest>,\n877\t) -> Result<Json<RotateScopedKeyResponse>, (StatusCode, String)>\n878\twhere\n879\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n880\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n881\t{\n882\t    let app_state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n883\t\n884\t    let redis = app_state.redis_store.clone().ok_or_else(|| {\n885\t        (\n886\t            StatusCode::PRECONDITION_FAILED,\n887\t            \"scoped key rotation requires Redis task store\".into(),\n888\t        )\n889\t    })?;\n890\t\n891\t    if !app_state.config.search_ui.enabled {\n892\t        return Err((\n893\t            StatusCode::PRECONDITION_FAILED,\n894\t            \"search_ui is not enabled\".into(),\n895\t        ));\n896\t    }\n897\t\n898\t    let rotation_state = ScopedKeyRotationState {\n899\t        config: app_state.config.clone(),\n900\t        redis,\n901\t        pod_id: app_state.pod_id.clone(),\n902\t    };\n903\t\n904\t    info!(\n905\t        index = %index,\n906\t        force = body.force,\n907\t        pod_id = %app_state.pod_id,\n908\t        \"manual scoped key rotation triggered\"\n909\t    );\n910\t\n911\t    match scoped_key_rotation::check_and_rotate(&rotation_state, &index, body.force).await {\n912\t        Ok(response) => Ok(Json(response)),\n913\t        Err(e) => {\n914\t            error!(index = %index, error = %e, \"manual scoped key rotation failed\");\n915\t            Err((StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, e))\n916\t        }\n917\t    }\n918\t}\n919\t\n920\t/// Parse a rate limit string like \"10/minute\" into (limit, window_seconds).\n921\tpub fn parse_rate_limit(s: &str) -> Result<(u64, u64), String> {\n922\t    let parts: Vec<&str> = s.split('/').collect();\n923\t    if parts.len() != 2 {\n924\t        return Err(format!(\"invalid rate limit format: '{}', expected 'N/UNIT'\", s));\n925\t    }\n926\t    let limit: u64 = parts[0].parse()\n927\t        .map_err(|_| format!(\"invalid limit number: '{}'\", parts[0]))?;\n928\t    let window_seconds = match parts[1] {\n929\t        \"second\" | \"s\" => 1,\n930\t        \"minute\" | \"m\" => 60,\n931\t        \"hour\" | \"h\" => 3600,\n932\t        \"day\" | \"d\" => 86400,\n933\t        unit => return Err(format!(\"invalid time unit: '{}', expected second/minute/hour/day\", unit)),\n934\t    };\n935\t    Ok((limit, window_seconds))\n936\t}\n937\t\n938\t/// Generate a random session ID.\n939\tfn generate_session_id() -> String {\n940\t    let mut bytes = [0u8; 24];\n941\t    rand::rngs::OsRng.fill_bytes(&mut bytes);\n942\t    hex::encode(&bytes)\n943\t}\n944\t\n945\t/// POST /_miroir/admin/login — admin login with rate limiting and exponential backoff.\n946\t///\n947\t/// Request body:\n948\t/// ```json\n949\t/// { \"admin_key\": \"...\" }\n950\t/// ```\n951\t///\n952\t/// On success, sets a `miroir_admin_session` cookie and returns:\n953\t/// ```json\n954\t/// { \"success\": true }\n955\t/// ```\n956\t///\n957\t/// Rate limiting (per source IP):\n958\t/// - 10 requests per minute (configurable via `admin_ui.rate_limit.per_ip`)\n959\t/// - After 5 consecutive failed attempts, exponential backoff applies:\n960\t///   - 10m, 20m, 40m, ... up to 24h cap\n961\t///\n962\t/// Successful login resets both the rate limit counter and backoff state.\n963\tpub async fn admin_login<S>(\n964\t    State(state): State<S>,\n965\t    headers: HeaderMap,\n966\t    Json(body): Json<AdminLoginRequest>,\n967\t) -> Response\n968\twhere\n969\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n970\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n971\t{\n972\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n973\t\n974\t    // Extract source IP from X-Forwarded-For or X-Real-IP (trust proxy)\n975\t    let source_ip = headers\n976\t        .get(\"x-forwarded-for\")\n977\t        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n978\t        .and_then(|s| s.split(',').next())\n979\t        .or_else(|| headers.get(\"x-real-ip\").and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok()))\n980\t        .unwrap_or(\"unknown\")\n981\t        .trim()\n982\t        .to_string();\n983\t\n984\t    // Parse rate limit config\n985\t    let (limit, window_seconds) = match parse_rate_limit(&state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.per_ip) {\n986\t        Ok(parsed) => parsed,\n987\t        Err(e) => {\n988\t            error!(error = %e, \"invalid admin_ui.rate_limit.per_ip config\");\n989\t            return (\n990\t                StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,\n991\t                Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n992\t                    success: false,\n993\t                    message: Some(\"Rate limit configuration error\".into()),\n994\t                }),\n995\t            ).into_response();\n996\t        }\n997\t    };\n998\t\n999\t    // Check rate limit and backoff\n1000\t    let backend = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backend.as_str();\n1001\t    if backend == \"redis\" {\n1002\t        if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n1003\t            match redis.check_rate_limit_admin_login(&source_ip, limit, window_seconds) {\n1004\t                Ok((allowed, wait_seconds)) => {\n1005\t                    if !allowed {\n1006\t                        if let Some(ws) = wait_seconds {\n1007\t                            warn!(\n1008\t                                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n1009\t                                wait_seconds = ws,\n1010\t                                \"admin login rate limited (backoff)\"\n1011\t                            );\n1012\t                            return (\n1013\t                                StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS,\n1014\t                                Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n1015\t                                    success: false,\n1016\t                                    message: Some(format!(\n1017\t                                        \"Too many failed login attempts. Try again in {} seconds.\",\n1018\t                                        ws\n1019\t                                    )),\n1020\t                                }),\n1021\t                            ).into_response();\n1022\t                        } else {\n1023\t                            return (\n1024\t                                StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS,\n1025\t                                Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n1026\t                                    success: false,\n1027\t                                    message: Some(\"Too many login attempts. Please try again later.\".into()),\n1028\t                                }),\n1029\t                            ).into_response();\n1030\t                        }\n1031\t                    }\n1032\t                    // Allowed, proceed\n1033\t                }\n1034\t                Err(e) => {\n1035\t                    error!(error = %e, \"failed to check admin login rate limit\");\n1036\t                    // Continue anyway on error (fail-open)\n1037\t                }\n1038\t            }\n1039\t        }\n1040\t    } else if backend == \"local\" {\n1041\t        // Local backend rate limiting\n1042\t        let (allowed, wait_seconds) = state.local_rate_limiter.check(\n1043\t            &source_ip,\n1044\t            limit,\n1045\t            window_seconds * 1000,\n1046\t            state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.failed_attempt_threshold,\n1047\t            state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_start_minutes,\n1048\t            state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_max_hours * 60,\n1049\t        );\n1050\t        if !allowed {\n1051\t            warn!(\n1052\t                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n1053\t                wait_seconds = ?wait_seconds,\n1054\t                \"admin login rate limited (local backend)\"\n1055\t            );\n1056\t            return (\n1057\t                StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS,\n1058\t                Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n1059\t                    success: false,\n1060\t                    message: if let Some(ws) = wait_seconds {\n1061\t                        Some(format!(\n1062\t                            \"Too many failed login attempts. Try again in {} seconds.\",\n1063\t                            ws\n1064\t                        ))\n1065\t                    } else {\n1066\t                        Some(\"Too many login attempts. Please try again later.\".into())\n1067\t                    },\n1068\t                }),\n1069\t            ).into_response();\n1070\t        }\n1071\t    }\n1072\t\n1073\t    // Verify admin_key (constant-time comparison to prevent timing side-channels)\n1074\t    use subtle::ConstantTimeEq as _;\n1075\t    if body.admin_key.as_bytes().ct_eq(state.config.admin.api_key.as_bytes()).into() {\n1076\t        // Successful login - reset rate limit counters\n1077\t        if backend == \"redis\" {\n1078\t            if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n1079\t                if let Err(e) = redis.reset_rate_limit_admin_login(&source_ip) {\n1080\t                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to reset admin login rate limit\");\n1081\t                }\n1082\t            }\n1083\t        } else if backend == \"local\" {\n1084\t            state.local_rate_limiter.reset(&source_ip);\n1085\t        }\n1086\t\n1087\t        // Generate session ID and seal it\n1088\t        let session_id = generate_session_id();\n1089\t        let sealed = match seal_session(&session_id, &state.seal_key) {\n1090\t            Ok(sealed) => sealed,\n1091\t            Err(e) => {\n1092\t                error!(error = %e, \"failed to seal admin session\");\n1093\t                return (\n1094\t                    StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,\n1095\t                    Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n1096\t                        success: false,\n1097\t                        message: Some(\"Failed to create session\".into()),\n1098\t                    }),\n1099\t                ).into_response();\n1100\t            }\n1101\t        };\n1102\t\n1103\t        info!(\n1104\t            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n1105\t            session_prefix = &session_id[..8],\n1106\t            \"admin login successful\"\n1107\t        );\n1108\t\n1109\t        // Set cookie and return success\n1110\t        (\n1111\t            StatusCode::OK,\n1112\t            [\n1113\t                (\"Set-Cookie\", format!(\"{}={}; HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite=Strict; Path=/; Max-Age={}\",\n1114\t                    COOKIE_NAME, sealed, state.config.admin_ui.session_ttl_s)),\n1115\t            ],\n1116\t            Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n1117\t                success: true,\n1118\t                message: None,\n1119\t            }),\n1120\t        ).into_response()\n1121\t    } else {\n1122\t        // Wrong admin_key - record failure for backoff tracking\n1123\t        warn!(\n1124\t            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n1125\t            \"admin login failed: invalid admin_key\"\n1126\t        );\n1127\t\n1128\t        if backend == \"redis\" {\n1129\t            if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n1130\t                let backoff_start_minutes = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_start_minutes;\n1131\t                let backoff_max_hours = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_max_hours;\n1132\t                let failed_threshold = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.failed_attempt_threshold;\n1133\t\n1134\t                if let Err(e) = redis.record_failure_admin_login(\n1135\t                    &source_ip,\n1136\t                    failed_threshold,\n1137\t                    backoff_start_minutes,\n1138\t                    backoff_max_hours,\n1139\t                ) {\n1140\t                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to record admin login failure\");\n1141\t                }\n1142\t            }\n1143\t        } else if backend == \"local\" {\n1144\t            let backoff_start_minutes = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_start_minutes;\n1145\t            let backoff_max_hours = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_max_hours;\n1146\t            let failed_threshold = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.failed_attempt_threshold;\n1147\t\n1148\t            state.local_rate_limiter.record_failure(\n1149\t                &source_ip,\n1150\t                failed_threshold,\n1151\t                backoff_start_minutes,\n1152\t                backoff_max_hours * 60,\n1153\t            );\n1154\t        }\n1155\t\n1156\t        (\n1157\t            StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED,\n1158\t            Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n1159\t                success: false,\n1160\t                message: Some(\"Invalid admin key\".into()),\n1161\t            }),\n1162\t        ).into_response()\n1163\t    }\n1164\t}\n1165\t\n1166\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n1167\t// Rebalancer Admin API Endpoints (plan §4)\n1168\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n1169\t\n1170\t/// POST /_miroir/nodes — Add a node to a replica group.\n1171\t///\n1172\t/// Request body:\n1173\t/// ```json\n1174\t/// {\n1175\t///   \"id\": \"node-new\",\n1176\t///   \"address\": \"http://node-new:7700\",\n1177\t///   \"replica_group\": 0\n1178\t/// }\n1179\t/// ```\n1180\t///\n1181\t/// Implements plan §2 \"Adding a node to an existing group\":\n1182\t/// 1. Add node to topology in `Joining` state\n1183\t/// 2. Send `NodeAdded` event to rebalancer worker\n1184\t/// 3. Worker computes affected shards and starts migration with leader lease\n1185\tpub async fn add_node<S>(\n1186\t    State(state): State<S>,\n1187\t    Json(body): Json<serde_json::Value>,\n1188\t) -> Result<Json<serde_json::Value>, (StatusCode, String)>\n1189\twhere\n1190\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n1191\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n1192\t{\n1193\t    let app_state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n1194\t\n1195\t    let id = body.get(\"id\")\n1196\t        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n1197\t        .ok_or_else(|| (StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, \"Missing 'id' field\".into()))?\n1198\t        .to_string();\n1199\t\n1200\t    let address = body.get(\"address\")\n1201\t        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n1202\t        .ok_or_else(|| (StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, \"Missing 'address' field\".into()))?\n1203\t        .to_string();\n1204\t\n1205\t    let replica_group = body.get(\"replica_group\")\n1206\t        .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n1207\t        .ok_or_else(|| (StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, \"Missing 'replica_group' field\".into()))?\n1208\t        as u32;\n1209\t\n1210\t    // Add node to topology\n1211\t    {\n1212\t        let mut topo = app_state.topology.write().await;\n1213\t        // Check if node already exists\n1214\t        let node_id = NodeId::new(id.clone());\n1215\t        if topo.node(&node_id).is_some() {\n1216\t            return Err((StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST,\n1217\t                format!(\"Node {} already exists\", id)));\n1218\t        }\n1219\t        // Check if replica group exists\n1220\t        let group_count = topo.groups().count() as u32;\n1221\t        if replica_group >= group_count {\n1222\t            return Err((StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST,\n1223\t                format!(\"Replica group {} does not exist\", replica_group)));\n1224\t        }\n1225\t        let node = Node::new(node_id, address, replica_group);\n1226\t        topo.add_node(node);\n1227\t    }\n1228\t\n1229\t    // Send event to rebalancer worker (if available)\n1230\t    if let Some(ref worker) = app_state.rebalancer_worker {\n1231\t        let event = TopologyChangeEvent::NodeAdded {\n1232\t            node_id: id.clone(),\n1233\t            replica_group,\n1234\t            index_uid: \"default\".to_string(),\n1235\t        };\n1236\t        if let Err(e) = worker.event_sender().try_send(event) {\n1237\t            error!(error = %e, node_id = %id, \"failed to send NodeAdded event to rebalancer worker\");\n1238\t            return Err((StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,\n1239\t                format!(\"Failed to queue rebalancing: {}\", e)));\n1240\t        }\n1241\t    }\n1242\t\n1243\t    info!(node_id = %id, replica_group, \"Node addition queued for rebalancing\");\n1244\t    Ok(Json(serde_json::json!({\n1245\t        \"node_id\": id,\n1246\t        \"replica_group\": replica_group,\n1247\t        \"message\": format!(\"Node {} added to replica group {}, rebalancing will start shortly\", id, replica_group),\n1248\t    })))\n1249\t}\n1250\t\n1251\t/// DELETE /_miroir/nodes/{id} — Remove a node from the cluster.\n1252\t///\n1253\t/// Request body (optional):\n1254\t/// ```json\n1255\t/// {\n1256\t///   \"force\": false  // Set to true to bypass draining check\n1257\t/// }\n1258\t/// ```\n1259\t///\n1260\t/// Requires the node to be in `draining` state unless `force=true`.\n1261\t/// Note: This only removes the node from topology. Draining must be completed first.\n1262\tpub async fn remove_node<S>(\n1263\t    State(state): State<S>,\n1264\t    Path(node_id): Path<String>,\n1265\t    Json(body): Json<serde_json::Value>,\n1266\t) -> Result<Json<serde_json::Value>, (StatusCode, String)>\n1267\twhere\n1268\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n1269\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n1270\t{\n1271\t    let app_state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n1272\t\n1273\t    let force = body.get(\"force\")\n1274\t        .and_then(|v| v.as_bool())\n1275\t        .unwrap_or(false);\n1276\t\n1277\t    let group\n1286\t        let group = topo.groups()\n1287\t            .find(|g| g.id == node.replica_group)\n1288\t            .ok_or_else(|| (StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, format!(\"Replica group {} not found\", node.replica_group)))?;\n1289\t\n1290\t        if group.nodes().len() <= 1 {\n1291\t            return Err((StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, \"Cannot remove the last node in a replica group\".into()));\n1292\t        }\n1293\t\n1294\t        node.status\n1295\t    };\n1296\t\n1297\t    if !force && node_status != miroir_core::topology::NodeStatus::Draining {\n1298\t        return Err((StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, format!(\n1299\t            \"Node {} is not in draining state (current: {:?}), use force=true to bypass\",\n1300\t            node_id, node_status\n1301\t        ).into()));\n1302\t    }\n1303\t\n1304\t    // Remove node from topology\n1305\t    {\n1306\t        let mut topo = app_state.topology.write().await;\n1307\t        topo.remove_node(&node_id_obj);\n1308\t    }\n1309\t\n1310\t    info!(node_id = %node_id, force, \"Node removal completed\");\n1311\t    Ok(Json(serde_json::json!({\n1312\t        \"node_id\": Mark node as `draining`\n1321\t/// 2. Send `NodeDraining` event to rebalancer worker\n1322\t/// 3. Worker computes shard destinations and starts migration with leader lease\n1323\tpub async fn drain_node<S>(\n1324\t    State(state): State<S>,\n1325\t    Path(node_id): Path<String>,\n1326\t) -> Result<Json<serde_json::Value>, (StatusCode, String)>\n1327\twhere\n1328\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n1329\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n1330\t{\n1331\t    let app_state = (StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE, \"Rebalancer worker not initialized\".into()))?;\n1336\t\n1337\t    // Get node info and mark as draining\n1338\t    let replica_group = {\n1339\t        let mut topo .find(|g| g.id == node.replica_group)\n1347\t            .ok_or_else(|| (StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, format!(\"Replica group {} not found\", node.replica_group)))?;\n1348\t\n1349\t        if migrations will start shortly\", node_id),\n1380\t    })))\n1381\t}\n1382\t\n1383\t/// POST /_miroir/nodes/{id}/fail — Mark a node as failed.\n1384\t///\n1385\t/// Marks a node as failed and sends a
